remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text: Now that the dust is settled, what’s really funny to me about the taste commentary is it’s coming from a LOT of people who never have built anything.. You can’t achieve (or refine) taste without being in the arena. It’s like a chef cooking and expecting their first dish to be good on the first go. But, when you give it a 100 iterations and maybe cook a 100 dishes, then the 101st one has imbued all the lessons from those 10,000 iterations. But, if you over fit without much variation, then people get tired of it since well they want something new as well. The best people are able to learn, imbue and innovate by breaking patterns consistently. It’ll be interesting to see how AI does the same - and unlike most people I think it has a fair shot at actually building taste. End rant.
remarks.translation: 尘埃落定后，关于品味的评论让我觉得真正好笑的是，这些评论来自很多从未真正建造过任何东西的人……不置身其中，你根本无法获得（或打磨）品味。这就像一位厨师做饭，指望第一道菜就能一次成功。但当你反复尝试100次，或许做了100道菜之后，第101道菜就会融入那10,000次迭代中的所有经验教训。然而，如果你过度拟合而缺乏变化，人们就会厌倦，因为他们也想要一些新东西。最优秀的人能够通过不断打破模式来学习、融入并创新。看看人工智能如何做到同样的事会很有趣——与大多数人不同，我认为它确实有相当的机会真正培养出品味。牢骚完毕。

remarks.name: Dan Shipper
remarks.role: ceo every
remarks.text: BREAKING: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol! As of today, by U.S. government directive, access is limited to only ~20 pre-approved companies and @every is not on the list. This appears to be a temporary situation while the government races to figure out a long-term policy for releasing frontier models with advanced capabilities. I understand and applaud the need for some government oversight in making American infrastructure resilient to cyberattacks and other potential new threats from the misuse of these models. However, I also strongly believe that widespread democratic access to frontier models is absolutely necessary to our country’s leading position in the AI race. It’s also critical for allowing American workers to keep pace with the new skills they need to be productive in this era. A world where advanced models are locked up only for use by the employees of AI giants and a select few companies is one where ambitious students, independent builders, and working professionals are denied the tools they need to learn, create, and compete to their fullest potential. Also, speaking for @every and the community of developers and writers who share early access with us: Our job is to test these tools early so that we can prepare Americans for how to use them to their fullest extent. If we lose access, we lose the ability to do that important job well. Thankfully, both OpenAI and the government seem to be working to make broad access available soon. We’ll be ready to vibe check when that happens :)
remarks.translation: 重磅消息：OpenAI 发布了 GPT-5.6 Sol！即日起，根据美国政府指令，该模型仅限约 20 家预先批准的公司使用，而 @every 并不在名单之列。这似乎是临时性安排，政府正加紧制定针对具备先进能力的前沿模型的长期发布政策。我理解并赞同政府需要实施一定监管，以增强美国基础设施抵御网络攻击及防范此类模型被滥用所带来的其他潜在新威胁。但我同样坚信，让前沿模型实现广泛的民主化使用，对于我国在人工智能竞赛中保持领先地位绝对至关重要。这也关系到美国劳动者能否及时掌握新时代所需的新技能，从而保持高效生产力。若先进模型仅被锁在 AI 巨头和少数几家公司的员工手中，那么胸怀大志的学生、独立开发者以及职场专业人士，都将失去学习、创造和充分施展才华所必需的工具。此外，我谨代表 @every 以及与我们共享早期访问权限的开发者与创作者社群发声：我们的职责是尽早测试这些工具，以便帮助美国民众做好充分准备，学会如何最大限度地运用它们。若失去访问权限，我们便无法出色完成这项重要使命。所幸的是，OpenAI 与政府似乎都在努力推动尽快实现广泛开放。届时，我们定会第一时间进行体验评测 :)

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: AI regulation is far less simple than it looks. It’s prisoners dilemma at insane scale. In theory if all leading AI labs globally agreed to the same process of review and slow down, then we’d get frontier intelligence at similar rates and it diffuses relatively evenly. If the US remains at the frontier at all times, and has heavy regulation on the release of intelligence then we end up with an economic and geopolitical edge because we can control who has access to frontier intelligence. If we delay model releases, however, and another player - specifically China - doesn’t slow down and has equally strong models (not now but soon?) then our delays end up advantaging their models and eventually their tech stack. Now, the US could ban these models, but that actually only puts the US at a steeper disadvantage because other countries won’t have those bans. Then, from a relative competitiveness standpoint the US has now fallen behind even though it started in front. So, none of this is as simple as it looks. At some point it’s a simple bet of can closed models remain at the frontier in perpetuity or is there a risk of any other player or market catching up or just not falling behind.
remarks.translation: 人工智能监管远非表面看起来那么简单，这本质上是一场规模惊人的囚徒困境。理论上，如果全球所有顶尖AI实验室都同意采用相同的审查流程并放慢研发速度，那么我们就能以相近的速度获得前沿智能，且其扩散会相对均衡。若美国始终处于技术前沿，并对智能技术的发布实施严格监管，我们就能通过控制前沿智能的获取权限来获得经济和地缘政治优势。然而，如果我们推迟模型发布，而其他参与者——特别是中国——并未放缓脚步，且拥有同样强大的模型（目前尚未实现，但或许很快？），那么我们的延迟反而会助长其模型优势，最终壮大其技术生态。美国固然可以禁用这些模型，但这只会让美国陷入更被动的境地，因为其他国家不会实施此类禁令。这样一来，从相对竞争力的角度看，即便美国最初领先，最终也会落后。因此，这一切远非表面那么简单。归根结底，这不过是一场简单赌局：封闭模型能否永远保持前沿地位，还是存在其他参与者或市场迎头赶上甚至不被甩开的风险。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: We now have de facto AI regulation. It’s not obvious why from here on out models that have certain levels of capability or are trained on certain compute sizes won’t have to be reviewed by the government before release. Realistically, as AI models became more and more powerful this was going to be inevitable (I think it’s too early, but here we are). So now it’s mostly just interesting to think about the implications and scenarios from here. A few would be: * America gets to control who gets access to frontier intelligence and when. This generally works as long as we remain at the frontier at all times and don’t have a risk of being surpassed. At the moment we have a clear lead in frontier intelligence so this is a good bet, but lots of motivated parties would love to change that. * This likely creates backlog of AI releases which means that we will see less rapid fire back and forth jumps in model progress. Bull/fine case is that we just get bigger step functions per release at a slower rate and we end up at the same point we would have. Bear case is those incremental smaller jumps were necessary for the continued flywheel of innovation. * Other countries likely have even more incentive to at least hedge their bets with sovereign AI strategies so aren’t dependent on access to US AI all times. Previously this was relatively moot because the alternative wasn’t good enough, but that could change out of necessity and what we’re seeing in China. * Open weights obviously a big winner here as it becomes what likely sovereign AI gets built out on, and what (for now) can still be released to the market without the same controls. One interesting question would be how regulation eventually extends to open models, which would have its own set of long term consequences. Anyway some big updates to everyone’s mental models of AI regulation as a result of the capabilities we’re now seeing in AI. Wild times.
remarks.translation:
我们如今已有了事实上的AI监管。从今往后，具备特定能力水平或基于特定算力规模训练的模型，在发布前很可能需要接受政府审查，这背后的原因并不难理解。现实来看，随着AI模型日益强大，这种局面本就难以避免（我认为为时过早，但事已至此）。所以现在，思考由此产生的各种影响和情景，反倒成了最值得玩味的事。其中几点包括：  
* 美国得以掌控谁能获取前沿智能、以及何时获取。只要我们能始终站在前沿、没有被他国赶超的风险，这套机制大体就能奏效。目前我们在前沿智能领域优势明显，因此这算是个稳妥的赌注，但许多雄心勃勃的势力正渴望改变这一格局。  
* 这很可能导致AI发布出现积压，意味着模型进展中那种快速交替的跳跃式迭代会减少。乐观/理想的情况是，每次发布带来的能力跃升幅度更大，只是节奏放缓，最终我们仍能抵达原本会达到的同一节点。悲观的情况则是，那些渐进式的小幅跃迁，对于维持创新的持续飞轮本是必不可少的。  
* 其他国家很可能更有动力至少通过主权AI战略来对冲风险，以免在所有时刻都依赖美国的AI供应。此前这基本无关紧要，因为替代方案不够好，但出于现实需要，以及我们在中国看到的情况，这种局面可能改变。  
* 开放权重模型显然是这里的大赢家，它很可能成为主权AI构建的基础，并且（就目前而言）仍能在不受同等管控的情况下向市场发布。一个有趣的问题是，监管最终将如何延伸到开放模型，而这会带来一系列长期后果。  
总之，鉴于我们如今在AI身上看到的能力，每个人对AI监管的思维模型都迎来了重大更新。真是疯狂的时代。

remarks.text: Scaling Laws, Carefully
remarks.content: Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases predictably as we scale up model size $N$, dataset size $D$, and compute $C$, following a power-law curve, which appears as a straight line on a log-log p
remarks.contentTranslation: 缩放定律是深度学习中最关键的经验性发现之一。其形式简洁明了：训练损失 $L$ 会随着模型规模 $N$、数据集大小 $D$ 和计算量 $C$ 的扩大而可预测地降低，遵循幂律曲线，在对数-对数坐标图上呈现为一条直线。
remarks.translation: 缩放定律，需谨慎

remarks.name: Dan Shipper
remarks.role: ceo every
remarks.text: Are we hurtling toward a future where AI can do everything humans can? Edwin Chen (@echen) believes we might be. He’s the CEO of Surge AI, one of the largest providers of expert data for frontier labs. Surge passed over $1 billion in revenue without raising any outside capital, and that gives Edwin a unique perspective on how quickly AI progress is accelerating. I’m on the record arguing that AI automation actually creates more human work. I also believe that even though AI progress is accelerating exponentially, we’re much farther away from AI replacing humans than it might seem. That’s why I had Edwin on @every’s AI & I. We batted around different visions of the future, and discussed whether humanity will retain its unique place in the universe, and what that might be. We get into: • If Chen’s version of the future materializes, he’s worried it’ll make people stop trying. One answer comes from a short story by science fiction writer Ted Chiang: Behave as if your decisions matter, even when you know they don’t. • AI may soon be able to take a nebulous goal like “win a Fields Medal” and execute. What it can’t do, I argue, is set its own goals—LLMs have no intrinsic motivation, no drive to explore, no ability to just change their mind. • A model optimized for engagement doesn’t provide the most valuable user experience. Edwin spent 20 rounds polishing a pointless email with one model before Claude told him to just send it. • Why AI is still bad at writing: models learn to hack the metrics they're trained on. Edwin's Hemingway Bench found models outputting a metaphor in every single sentence, an overindexxing that makes for a terrible reading experience. This is a must-watch for anyone interested in where we fit as models get more capable. Watch below! Timestamps 1. Introduction: 00:00:54 2. Surge as a "school for AGI": 00:01:49 3. What AI's capacity for novel mathematics says about human achievement: 00:04:46 4. Motivation in an era when AI can do everything: 00:07:29 5. The trap of optimizing AI models for engagement: 00:14:34 6. Training using datasets versus training using environments: 00:29:34 7. The value of personal data: 00:35:09 8. Why models are bad at writing: 00:39:40 9. Chen's AGI timeline: 00:42:00
remarks.translation: 我们是否正飞速驶向一个AI无所不能的未来？Edwin Chen（@echen）认为可能如此。他是Surge AI的首席执行官，这家公司是前沿实验室最大的专家数据提供商之一。Surge在没有筹集任何外部资金的情况下，营收突破了$1 billion，这让Edwin对AI进展的加速有着独特的视角。我曾公开主张，AI自动化实际上会创造更多人类工作。我也认为，尽管AI进步呈指数级加速，但我们距离AI取代人类可能比看上去要遥远得多。这就是我邀请Edwin做客@every的AI & I节目的原因。我们围绕不同的未来愿景展开交锋，探讨人类是否会在宇宙中保留其独特地位，以及那可能是什么。我们深入讨论了： • 如果Chen所设想的未来成真，他担心这会让人停止尝试。一个答案来自科幻作家Ted Chiang的短篇小说：即使知道自己的决定无足轻重，也要表现得好像它们至关重要。 • AI可能很快就能接手一个模糊的目标，比如“赢得菲尔兹奖”，并付诸执行。但我认为，它无法做到的是设定自己的目标——大语言模型没有内在动机，没有探索的驱动力，也没有随意改变想法的能力。 • 一个为参与度优化的模型，并不会提供最有价值的用户体验。Edwin用一个模型打磨一封毫无意义的邮件，来回修改了20轮，最后Claude告诉他直接发送算了。 • 为什么AI在写作上依然糟糕：模型学会了钻营训练指标的漏洞。Edwin的Hemingway Bench测试发现，模型会在每个句子中都输出一个隐喻，这种过度索引导致了极差的阅读体验。 对于任何关心在模型能力日益增强时我们自身定位的人来说，这期节目不容错过。请看下方视频！ 时间戳 1. 引言：00:00:54 2. Surge作为“AGI的学校”：00:01:49 3. AI的新数学能力对人类成就的启示：00:04:46 4. AI无所不能时代的动力：00:07:29 5. 为参与度优化AI模型的陷阱：00:14:34 6. 使用数据集训练与使用环境训练：00:29:34 7. 个人数据的价值：00:35:09 8. 为什么模型不擅长写作：00:39:40 9. Chen的AGI时间线：00:42:00

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Good post on the dynamics of AI pricing right now. We’re basically going to see a barbell dynamic on pricing between high cost frontier models and cheap but good open or closed weights models. Then the question is how do you maximize efficiency from the frontier models, and get better quality and performance of the cheaper models. That’s where the applied AI layer comes in. The applied layer can route to the best model at any time given the workload at hand, as well as mitigate either high token costs or worse performance. The closer you are to the underlying workflow, the better you can tune the use of models for a given business process. That’s all about understanding the workflows, domain specific FDEs, evals by customer or process, getting data setup well for agents, and more. Overall good for applied AI.
remarks.translation: 关于当前AI定价动态的好文章。我们基本上会看到定价呈现杠铃式格局——一端是高成本的前沿模型，另一端是廉价但优质的开放或封闭权重模型。接下来的问题是如何最大化前沿模型的效率，并提升廉价模型的质量与性能。这正是应用AI层发挥作用的地方。应用层可以根据当前工作负载随时调用最佳模型，同时缓解高昂的令牌成本或性能不佳的问题。你越贴近底层工作流程，就越能为特定业务流程优化模型的使用。这一切都关乎理解工作流程、特定领域的FDE、按客户或流程进行的评估、为智能体妥善设置数据等等。总体而言，这对应用AI是有利的。

remarks.text: Tag Claude in a channel, it spins up an instance with its own sandbox. It clones repos, writes code, tests, compiles all in that isolated environment and the sandbox gets thrown away when it's done. One instance per thread, its own memory and permissions per channel.

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: I’ve had the chance to see this ecosystem from the different sides - enterprises, agent startups, data providers, labs. The core tension here is that the business models, moats, value exchange, execution playbooks for all players are being figured out in real time. This debate around token pricing is really a debate over where value will accrue - models, application layer, distribution or other layers. Expect a lot of strong opinions from the different sides to shape the narrative in their favor.
remarks.translation: 我有机会从不同角度观察这个生态系统——企业、智能体初创公司、数据提供商、实验室。这里的核心矛盾在于，所有参与者的商业模式、护城河、价值交换和执行策略都在实时摸索中。围绕代币定价的争论，本质上是在争论价值将流向何处——模型层、应用层、分发层还是其他层面。预计各方都会提出大量强硬观点，试图将叙事导向对自己有利的方向。

remarks.text: AI explainability in finance: auditable models, GenAI, and agents
remarks.content: Regulators are asking financial institutions a direct question: Can you explain how your AI made that decision? For most, the honest answer is no — not fully, not on demand, and not across every system that now influences regulated decisions. Compliance functions are expected to cover
remarks.contentTranslation: 监管机构正向金融机构提出一个直截了当的问题：你能解释你的AI是如何做出那个决定的吗？对大多数机构而言，诚实的答案是“不能”——无法完全解释、无法按需解释、也无法覆盖所有影响受监管决策的系统。合规职能被要求涵盖
remarks.translation: 金融领域的人工智能可解释性：可审计模型、生成式AI与智能体

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Almost all AI model and agent progress is downstream from evals. Open weights post training for specific domains comes down to evals. Agent improvements in the applied AI layer is all about evals. Agentic enterprise deployments that actually can augment work is all about evals. It’s all evals. This will become a core competency of any enterprise in the future. The companies that are able to best understand their own (and/or customers) workflows and how well agents participate in that work will be in the best position to actually drive real automation.
remarks.translation: 几乎所有AI模型与智能体的进步都源于评估。针对特定领域的开源权重后训练，归根结底在于评估。应用AI层中智能体的改进，核心仍是评估。真正能辅助工作的企业级智能体部署，关键还是评估。一切皆评估。这将成为未来任何企业的核心竞争力。那些最能理解自身（及/或客户）工作流程、并精准衡量智能体参与程度的企业，将占据实现真正自动化的最佳位置。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text: A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc): Is your input (the context) longer than the output? I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output. If your input is much shorter than the output, it's almost certainly going to produce slop
remarks.translation: 防止AI生成垃圾内容（在写作、设计等领域）的一个实用经验法则是：你的输入（上下文）是否比输出更长？我发现，要让AI产出高质量结果，我的输入长度通常是输出的3到5倍。如果你的输入远短于输出，那么几乎可以肯定它会生成垃圾内容。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out the model that best performs the task. “Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically. It solves tasks directly when that is enough, or coordinates a team of expert models when a problem calls for more. The complexity of a multi-agent system never reaches your code.” This is generally how applied AI products are building their agent harnesses at this point, but the idea of making this an LLM that any developer can interact with is also a great idea. As we get more innovation with both frontier closed and OSS models, there’s going to be a ton of value produced for the layer that can route the best.
remarks.translation: 又有一个推动AI架构发展的新思路。Sakana发布了一款模型，能有效整合多种模型来完成任务。你只需调用一个API，系统就会自动将工作分配给最适合执行该任务的模型。“Fugu能自动完成模型选择、任务委派、验证和综合处理。简单任务直接解决，复杂问题则协调专家模型团队协作。多智能体系统的复杂性完全不会侵入你的代码。”这基本就是当前应用型AI产品构建智能体框架的方式，但将其打造成任何开发者都能交互的大语言模型，同样是个绝妙创意。随着前沿闭源模型和开源模型的持续创新，能够实现最优路由的中间层将产生巨大价值。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text: Why HTML turned out to be the foundation for agentic video making from @liu8in: “We’ve been trying to build a video agent. remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Pretty remarkable what’s happening with open weights AI right now. We’re seeing models achieve SOTA results on specific tasks, and getting close to frontier on some areas of coding and other domains. The more that open weights is able to maintain only a marginal gap from the frontier, instead of a widening gap, the more value that can be created with AI. Incidentally, this is actually fine for the frontier labs as well; if we can lower the cost of an overall task then AI usage goes up in general. You’re still likely using frontier models for planning, orchestration, reviewing, and other parts of work. But this is all very good for the applied layer of AI, which is now in a great position to cost optimize workloads with cheaper models or use tailored open models post-trained for specific tasks to improve performance.
remarks.translation: 开源权重AI的进展确实令人瞩目。我们看到模型在特定任务上达到顶尖水平，并在编程等领域的某些方面逼近前沿水准。开源权重与前沿模型的差距若能持续收窄而非扩大，AI创造的价值将愈发可观。有趣的是，这对前沿实验室同样有利——若能降低整体任务成本，AI的总体使用量就会上升。你仍可能使用前沿模型进行规划、协调、审核等工作环节，但这对AI应用层极为有利：现在完全可以通过更廉价的模型优化工作负载成本，或使用针对特定任务后训练的开源模型来提升性能。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text: The biggest problem with AI is that priors need to be reset every few weeks.. and it seems like most people are incapable of doing that. I talk to so many people who say xyz doesn’t work and when I ask when was the last time they tried testing it, the answer is always “a few months ago.” Brother that’s like eons ago in AI timelines. This is why each person needs to have their own evals of hard tasks and weekly tinker time to truly understand where the frontier is. And then you need to talk to enterprise buyers weekly who are usually two years behind. But they are the buyers so you need to understand how to market. The combination of the two will give you a pretty good sense of where to invest time and capital. If you just do these two weekly, you’ll be ahead of 99% of people.
remarks.translation: AI 最大的问题在于，先验认知每隔几周就需要重置……而大多数人似乎做不到这一点。我跟很多人聊过，他们说某某功能不行，我问他们上次测试是什么时候，答案总是“几个月前”。兄弟，在 AI 的时间尺度上，这简直像过了几个世纪。这就是为什么每个人都需要建立自己对高难度任务的评估体系，并每周留出动手探索的时间，才能真正理解技术前沿在哪里。然后，你还需要每周与企业采购方交流，他们通常落后两年。但他们是买单的人，所以你必须懂得如何营销。将这两者结合，你就能相当清晰地判断该把时间和资金投向何处。只要每周坚持做这两件事，你就会领先 99% 的人。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: The Product role is having an identity crisis too. Engineering has found its AI-native interface - SWE agents dramatically increase individual leverage. Companies are asking PMs to use AI, but they haven't evolved the role. So there are two camps. The old-school PM. AI accelerates the old job: more PRDs, more strategy decks, more docs. Lots of output, not much judgment. The Builder PM. Builder PMs use AI to expand their role across the product lifecycle. They explore a much larger surface area of ideas to arrive at the best. They run agents for market and user research, query logs and analytics directly, generate competing ideas, then curate the best ones. They constantly evolve their workflows to take advantage of the latest in AI. Their outputs are increasingly prototypes rather than docs - engineers react far more constructively to demos than docs. They do this all without compromising on the craft -they still form a strong point of view on what should be built and why. I think the role is moving much closer to Builder PMs.
remarks.translation: 产品经理这个角色也正面临身份危机。工程团队已经找到了AI原生的交互方式——SWE智能体大幅提升了个人效能。企业要求产品经理使用AI，却未推动这个角色的进化。于是形成了两个阵营。传统派产品经理。AI加速了旧有工作模式：更多产品需求文档、更多战略方案、更多文档。产出量激增，但判断力并未提升。构建派产品经理。这类产品经理运用AI将职能拓展至整个产品生命周期。他们探索更广阔的概念领域以筛选最优解，运用智能体进行市场与用户研究，直接查询日志与分析数据，生成竞争性方案，再精选最佳构想。他们持续迭代工作流程，充分利用最新AI技术。他们的产出日益转向原型而非文档——工程师对演示的反馈远比文档更具建设性。这一切都未牺牲专业素养——他们依然对构建什么、为何构建保持着鲜明主张。我认为这个角色正加速向构建派产品经理演进。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text: I used to be a die-hard Claude Code user. Codex has won me over because: → GPT-5.5 is excellent → Fast mode + generous limits = more reps → Little touches like steering, auto remote control on phone, etc But most of all Codex's browser and computer use capabilities are simply goated. I built so many workflows relying on those two things alone instead of hunting for APIs. I still use Claude Code too. The app seems to be getting better and the design and frontend capability of Opus is still much better than GPT. Whenever Fable comes back that's another reason to go back. Honestly, I hope these two compete forever and other players (Cursor/Grok, Gemini, etc) all stay competitive. This way the builder keeps winning 🙂
remarks.translation:
我以前是Claude Code的铁杆用户，但现在Codex把我征服了，原因如下：  
→ GPT-5.5 非常出色  
→ 快速模式 + 宽松的用量限制 = 更多实操机会  
→ 一些贴心细节，比如引导功能、手机端自动远程控制等  
但最重要的是，Codex的浏览器和电脑操控能力简直无敌。我靠这两项功能就搭建了无数工作流，根本不用到处找API。  
Claude Code我也还在用。这个应用似乎在不断改进，而且Opus的设计和前端能力依然远胜GPT。等Fable回归，那又是一个回去的理由。  
说实话，我希望这两家永远竞争下去，其他选手（Cursor/Grok、Gemini等）也都保持竞争力。这样，我们这些构建者就能一直受益 🙂

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text: The next hot programming language is… markdown. A minimal eve agent: 📂 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/ 📄 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍 📂 𝚜𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕𝚜/ 📄 𝚢𝚘𝚞𝚛-𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚝𝚒𝚜𝚎.𝚖𝚍 Deployable in one command: 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕. It’s the most accessible programming has ever been. And likely will ever be, at least for the generation of software fully defined and controlled by us humans. (As a fun fact, one of the initial prototypes for eve was codenamed 𝚕𝚊𝚜𝚝 by @timolins, both in homage to ‘@nextjs for agents’ but also in recognition of how enduring eve’s design feels to us.)
remarks.translation: 下一个热门编程语言是……Markdown。一个极简的Eve智能体：📂 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/ 📄 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍 📂 𝚜𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕𝚜/ 📄 𝚢𝚘𝚞𝚛-𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚝𝚒𝚜𝚎.𝚖𝚍 一条命令即可部署：𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕。这是编程史上最易上手的时刻，很可能也是未来人类完全定义和掌控软件时代中，最易上手的时刻。（有趣的是，Eve的早期原型之一曾被@timolins代号为𝚕𝚊𝚜𝚝，既致敬“面向智能体的@nextjs”，也体现了我们对其设计持久性的认可。）

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: The main variable in getting success with agents is whether you can get the agent the context it needs to do its work; and a major factor in that is if you can create a shared working area for that agent that a human can understand as well. This is one of the reasons why agents using file systems is such a big deal. It creates a unified system that both the person and the agent can work within to pass around data. “What they need is a working set: plans, notes, task lists, policies, drafts, summaries, logs, corrections, decisions, etc. For that layer, a filesystem-shaped interface tends to be more legible to both the model and the humans supervising it.” It turns out giving agents access to the systems we already know how to use, but in a way that is best optimized for them, is the perfect primitive for agents to work.
remarks.translation: 让智能体成功运作的关键变量，在于能否为其提供完成任务所需的上下文；而其中一个重要因素，是能否为智能体创建一个人类也能理解的共享工作区。这正是基于文件系统的智能体如此重要的原因之一——它构建了一个人与智能体都能在其中传递数据的统一系统。"它们需要的是一个工作集：计划、笔记、任务清单、策略、草稿、摘要、日志、修正记录、决策等。对于这一层，文件系统形态的界面往往对模型和监管人员都更具可读性。"事实证明，让智能体以最适合它们的方式访问我们早已熟悉的系统，正是其工作的完美基础单元。

remarks.text: The fact that open weights models are being discussed credibly at this level of capability should be a huge update for many. The implications of open models getting to frontier performance ensures that you can always have sovereign AI, have the ability to post train for your specific workflows, cost optimize for various workloads, and actually afford to do much more with AI (which opens up meaningfully different applications). Huge win for the applied AI layer.

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: This is a good update for getting access to Fable. It also gives us a view into what the future is likely going to look like with AI regulation. The government will have frameworks that are used to determine future model releases past a certain threshold of capability or compute levels. Given all the constituents involved, and the economic and societal significance of AI, this was practically an inevitability. It may seem small but the implications are massive. It will mean that each model update will go through an extensive review, testing, and feedback process. And in that processes lots of groups will weigh in on the risk of the model, and there will be lots of subjectivity on what the actual risks are or practicalities of exploiting those risks. A positive potential future here would be we still get massive model progress but they just happen in bigger jumps at once, where the labs pack in major improvements since the cost and slow down of each review stacks up. On the other side, the risk is that past a certain threshold we may not get to see the rapid back and forth of model progress that we’ve gotten used to which can have negative compounding effects. Hoping for the former outcome.
remarks.translation: 这是获取Fable使用权限的一个积极进展，也让我们得以窥见未来AI监管的可能走向。政府将建立评估框架，用于判定超过特定能力或算力门槛的后续模型发布。考虑到涉及的各方利益，以及AI在经济和社会层面的重大意义，这种监管几乎不可避免。看似微小的举措实则影响深远——这意味着每个模型更新都将经历严格的审查、测试和反馈流程。在此过程中，众多群体会就模型风险发表意见，而对实际风险或风险利用可行性的判断将充满主观性。乐观的未来图景是：我们仍能见证模型的重大突破，只是这些突破会以更大跨度的跃迁形式出现，实验室会将重大改进打包发布，因为每次审查累积的成本和延迟效应会促使他们这样做。而风险在于，超过某个临界点后，我们可能再也看不到已习以为常的模型快速迭代，这种停滞可能产生负面连锁效应。但愿前一种情况成为现实。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Nan Yu
remarks.role: head of product linear
remarks.text: It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from users. That approach ended up creating a bunch of slop, because users disengaged their brain from the process of writing an update. By switching to a multi turn interactive mode, the agent prompts you and asks you what to emphasize, what's most important, what context is missing, etc. This simple change elicits much better project updates and ensures that the user has a chance to steer rather than simply being asked to say yes to everything. A very good example of introducing friction for a net benefit.
remarks.translation: 我们经历了许多次迭代才找到合适的模式。早期我们非常天真，以为智能体无需用户过多输入就能一次性生成项目更新。这种方法最终制造了大量废料，因为用户在撰写更新时完全停止了思考。转而采用多轮交互模式后，智能体会主动提示你，询问需要强调什么、什么最重要、缺少哪些背景信息等。这个简单的改变能催生质量高得多的项目更新，并确保用户有机会主导方向，而不是仅仅被要求对所有内容点头称是。这是引入适当阻力以换取整体收益的绝佳范例。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: The past couple months we may be witnessing what the Applied AI layer will look like at scale. Despite some of the initial critique that this would just be a thin layer on the LLM, it’s turning out that actually driving agentic workflows in an enterprise is far more complex. And anywhere there’s complexity you generally gain a moat and value over time. Here are a few of the components that appear to make up the playbook based on the examples we’re collectively seeing in coding, legal, healthcare, customer support, financial services and other fields: * Build the features that bridge the gap between the intelligence and the workflow. Some workflows can be automated by simply going to a general purpose interface, but others need tuned interfaces and features tied to the work they’re augmenting or automating. They need features that are specific to capturing the kind of data that’s needed as context for the agent. And they need a variety of bespoke tools for the agent to use, and unique interfaces for the human-in-the-loop UX. Going far deeper than just presenting the output tokens is clearly critical, and the more depth there is here definitionally the more sustaining value. * Act as the model router balancing frontier intelligence with cheaper models. A natural advantage that any model neutral platform has is that it can naturally (in a business model-aligned way) leverage whatever level of intelligence is necessary for the workflows they’re automating to get done. There are plenty of scenarios where you need GPT-5.5 or Fable level capability, and also lots of workloads where a more efficient closed or open weights do the trick. Only the companies that have deep evals on specific tasks across all models, and the ability business model wise to leverage them, are in a great position. * Drive the actual implementation and change management via FDE or equivalent. A big reason the applied layer works at scale is that most enterprises need some degree of help and support with change management in implementing agents for their workflows. Data has to be cleaned up and moved to modern systems, processes have to be re-engineered and documented, workflows have to be evaled, SLAs have to get achieved, and so on. All of this is going to be unique for every type of process that gets implemented, which means the companies that have expertise in a given domain and come with all the relevant best practices will be in a strong position. * Implement domain specific GTM that creates expertise in that field. Beyond FDEs the companies that can build sales and GTM motions aligned to their domains also have a natural advantage. Most IT and line of business leaders have too many things to do in any given day; so if you’re not on their agenda, likely someone else is. Depending on the industry, there are entirely different sets of language you use, ways of working through security and compliance, regulatory controls you have to support, industry events that companies convene at, different system integrator and consulting partners you need to work with, and so on. The more generalized this gets the less you can speak the customers language, which is where the applied layer has a leg up. A final note. There remains a view that a lot of this is all mitigated by model intelligence alone, and the bitter lesson solves all of this in the limit. That’s possibly true, but enterprises need help changing *today*. And many aspects of how to bring intelligence to real world work don’t only depend on the axis of the pure capability of the model, so most of what you’re doing now to win ends up being important no matter how good the models get.
remarks.translation:
过去几个月，我们或许正在见证应用型AI层规模化后的样貌。尽管最初有人批评这不过是大型语言模型上的一层薄壳，但事实证明，在企业中驱动智能体工作流的复杂程度远超预期。而任何存在复杂性的地方，通常都会随时间积累起护城河与价值。根据我们在编程、法律、医疗、客服、金融服务等领域的集体观察，以下要素似乎构成了这套方法论的核心组件：

* 构建弥合智能与工作流差距的功能。有些工作流只需通用界面即可实现自动化，但另一些则需要针对其增强或替代的具体工作，定制化界面与功能。它们需要专门捕获智能体所需上下文数据的功能，需要为智能体配备各种定制工具，以及为人机协同体验设计独特的交互界面。显然，远比单纯呈现输出结果更深入的构建至关重要，而这里的深度越深，其持久价值就越明确。

* 充当模型路由器，平衡前沿智能与低成本模型。任何模型中立平台的自然优势在于，它能以符合商业模式的方式，灵活调用工作流自动化所需的任何智能层级。许多场景确实需要GPT-5.5或Fable级别的能力，但也有大量工作负载用更高效的闭源或开源模型即可胜任。唯有那些能对所有模型在特定任务上进行深度评估，并在商业模式上具备灵活调用能力的公司，才真正占据优势。

* 通过FDE或等效机制推动实际落地与变革管理。应用层能规模化运作的一个重要原因，是大多数企业在部署智能体工作流时，都需要一定程度的变革管理支持。数据必须清理并迁移至现代系统，流程需要重新设计与文档化，工作流需经过评估，服务水平协议必须达成等等。每个实施流程的独特性，意味着在特定领域拥有专业知识并掌握所有相关最佳实践的公司，将处于强势地位。

* 实施领域专属的市场进入策略，构建行业专长。除了FDE，能围绕自身领域构建销售与市场进入机制的公司也具备天然优势。大多数IT和业务线负责人每天事务繁多，若你不在他们的议程上，自然会有其他人占据位置。不同行业有着截然不同的沟通语言、安全合规处理方式、需支持的监管控制、行业会议聚集地、需合作的系统集成商与咨询伙伴等。越是泛化，就越难用客户的语言对话，而这正是应用层能占据优势的地方。

最后补充一点。仍有人认为，这一切终将被模型智能本身所消解，"苦涩的教训"最终会解决所有问题。这或许成立，但企业需要的是*当下*的改变。而将智能引入现实工作的诸多方面，并不完全取决于模型纯粹能力的单一维度，因此无论模型变得多强大，你现在为赢得市场所做的大部分努力，最终都至关重要。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text: Don't use AI for writing until you develop your own taste and voice Using AI to write isn't inherently bad. The danger is using AI to write before you've developed your taste for what is good content. If the AI produces slop, you won't even recognize it as slop Read a lot to figure out what good looks like. Write a lot to know what your voice sounds like. Only then, use AI to help you write, but make sure it actually sounds like you
remarks.translation: 在形成自己的品味和风格之前，不要用AI写作。用AI写作本身并非坏事。但危险在于，在你尚未培养出辨别优质内容的能力之前就依赖AI。如果AI生成的是垃圾内容，你甚至无法识别出它是垃圾。多阅读，才能明白什么是好的内容。多写作，才能了解自己的声音是什么样的。只有在那之后，才能借助AI辅助写作，但务必确保它听起来确实像你。

remarks.text: The thing about vibe coded personal apps: Building the thing takes a day. Finding out if you'll actually use it takes a week. Most of my dead projects worked fine. I just never opened them. Most products are built for a person who doesn't exist. Someone who remembers to open the app, clicks the right button, does step 1, 2, 3 every day. Real humans are lazy & forgetful. Build for that person instead
remarks.translation: 关于氛围编码的个人应用，有件事很真实：构建应用只需一天，但搞清楚自己是否真的会用，却要花上一周。我那些半途而废的项目，大多功能完好，只是我从未再打开过。多数产品是为一个不存在的人打造的——一个记得打开应用、点对按钮、每天按部就班完成步骤1、2、3的人。真实的人类既懒惰又健忘。请为那样的人去构建产品吧。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Governance as acceleration: data proves it’s not a speed bump
remarks.content: For years, the enterprise AI governance conversation has been shaped by a quiet assumption: Governance is the thing that slows AI down. This may look like the review cycle that delays the launch, committee that questions the model, or even documentation requirement that sits between a wo
remarks.contentTranslation: 多年来，企业AI治理的讨论一直受一种潜在假设的影响：治理是拖慢AI发展的因素。这或许表现为延迟上线的审查周期、质疑模型的委员会，甚至是横亘在愿景与落地之间的文档要求。
remarks.translation: 治理即加速：数据证明它并非减速带

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: The real prize in the SpaceX-Cursor deal is the agentic harness that will become the core for automating all knowledge work at scale. Here’s what SpaceX is getting: 1. Production-grade agentic harness -planning, context management, tool use, iteration, verification, memory, error recovery. Any product experience can be completely redesigned in an AI-native way with this harness. 2. Expertise on the full AI stack - model, evals, harness, application layer. 3. End to end product lifecycle focus: product strategy -> user journeys -> GTM. Everything optimized for one job: helping software engineers build. Very few companies do even one of these well. Cursor brings all three.
remarks.translation: SpaceX 与 Cursor 交易中真正的宝藏，是那个将成为规模化自动化所有知识工作核心的智能体驾驭框架。SpaceX 将获得的是：1. 生产级智能体驾驭框架——涵盖规划、上下文管理、工具使用、迭代、验证、记忆、错误恢复。借助这一框架，任何产品体验都能以 AI 原生的方式彻底重塑。2. 全栈 AI 领域的专长——覆盖模型、评估、驾驭框架、应用层。3. 端到端的产品生命周期聚焦：产品策略 -> 用户旅程 -> 市场进入。一切优化只为做好一件事：帮助软件工程师构建。很少有公司能在其中哪怕一个方面做到出色。Cursor 三者兼备。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: The Cursor deal is symbolically quite significant. It was effectively the first mega success in the applied layer of AI. They firmly proved out the value proposition of having a deep domain focus, the role you play as a model router, when to lean into frontier models vs. when to train your own, and the role of applied AI GTM and distribution to make sure you’re actually taking advantage of the market opportunity. Every aspect of their business was tuned to carve out ground and keep doubling down in a highly competitive space. This is really the first at scale template for how to execute this playbook.
remarks.translation: Cursor 的这笔交易具有相当重要的象征意义。它实质上是人工智能应用层领域的首个巨大成功。他们充分验证了深度聚焦特定领域的价值主张，明确了作为模型路由器的角色定位，掌握了何时依赖前沿模型、何时自行训练的时机判断，以及应用层人工智能的市场推广与分发策略在把握市场机遇中的关键作用。其业务的每个环节都经过精心调校，旨在激烈竞争中开辟阵地并持续加倍投入。这真正是首个规模化执行这套策略的范本。

remarks.text: A sandbox A function A server A build Are you getting it!? These are all expressions of the same underlying compute infrastructure. With tweaks to load balancing, concurrency, persistence, overcommit… 2026 is the year serverless and servers finally converge. With no gotchas
remarks.translation: 一个沙箱、一个函数、一台服务器、一次构建——你明白了吗！？这些其实都是同一套底层计算基础设施的不同表现形式。通过对负载均衡、并发、持久化、超分等机制的微调……2026年，无服务器与服务器将最终走向融合，没有任何陷阱。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: It’s very easy to say “we need an FDA for AI” or some equivalent government agency. Well this is what that would look like. The capabilities of AI models have near infinite permutations. It’s going to be very hard have a purely objective set of metrics that can be universally applied before every model release, without extended back and forth, research, and debate between model labs, academics, and the government. Now, imagine this same process with every country that you’re doing business with, globally, for every single model release. Add in a backlog of dozens of AI model releases, and you can quickly see how in the limit this will dramatically slow down all AI progress. This is why we need to primarily focus on regulating the applied uses of AI, where the risk actually shows up.
remarks.translation: 说“我们需要一个AI领域的FDA”或类似的政府机构很容易。但实际情况会是这样的。AI模型的能力几乎有无穷无尽的排列组合。要想在每次模型发布前，都有一套纯粹客观、能普遍适用的衡量标准，而不需要模型实验室、学术界和政府之间进行漫长的反复沟通、研究和辩论，这将非常困难。现在，再想象一下，在全球范围内，你与每个有业务往来的国家，对每一次模型发布都要经历同样的流程。再加上数十个AI模型发布积压待审，你很快就能看到，这最终将如何极大地拖慢所有AI的进步。这就是为什么我们需要主要关注对AI实际应用场景的监管，因为风险正是在那里显现的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text: One of our most requested features, longer Vercel function runtime, is here. What looks like an innocent tweak of a constant… is actually the conclusion a multi-year compute platform investment. Builds, Sandbox, and now Functions run on our homegrown microVM-based Fluid compute infrastructure. This investment has enabled innovations like function multi-concurrency, Active CPU pricing, and Secure Compute for private connectivity to existing cloud workloads… with lots more to come, in fact, in the coming days and weeks 😎
remarks.translation: 我们最常被要求的功能之一——更长的 Vercel 函数运行时间——现已上线。这看似只是对某个常量的简单调整，实则是我们多年计算平台投入的成果。构建、沙盒，以及现在的函数，都已运行在我们自研的基于 microVM 的 Fluid 计算基础设施上。这项投入催生了函数多并发、活跃 CPU 定价、以及用于与现有云工作负载建立私有连接的安全计算等创新……未来还有更多功能即将推出，事实上，就在接下来的几天和几周内 😎

remarks.text: havent seen many people outside anthropic ultracode yet. this thing is scarily good at burning tokens but you need to set up your repo to parallelize properly to make use of the fanout that i think subagents are best at. basically the idea is "subroutines but intelligent". when you undersatnd just how much knowledge work is just yakshaves after yakshaves that require some judgment and intelligence, you start to appreciate that dynamic workflows are not just for coding tasks...
remarks.translation: 目前除了Anthropic的Ultracode之外，还没看到太多人在用这个。这东西烧token的能力强得吓人，但你需要把仓库设置好，做好并行化，才能充分利用我认为子代理最擅长的扇出机制。基本上，这个想法就是“智能化的子程序”。当你意识到知识工作中有多少不过是需要判断力和智力的琐碎杂务时，你就会开始理解动态工作流不仅仅适用于编码任务……

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Great post. The companies that are able to get their unique IP, institutional knowledge, and data into a format and architecture that lets them capture all of the gains and progress in AI are going to be in the best position in the future. “the real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. You can offload a task, or even a job, but you can never offload your learning. The future of the firm is the ability to compound that learning across people and AI. This requires a new architectural approach where every business is able to build agentic systems that improve over time, while still retaining control over their IP. A company should be able to switch out a “generalist” model without losing the “company veteran” expertise built into their learning system.” We’re all collectively figuring out the right architecture for the future of AI. But it’s clear that so much of the power and value will accrue to wherever can best leverage any AI system against their information. This is also why the applied AI layer will also gain so much value over the coming years.
remarks.translation: 精彩好文。那些能将自身独特知识产权、机构知识和数据，转化为特定格式与架构，从而充分把握人工智能所有进步与成果的企业，未来将占据最有利的位置。"真正的机遇不在于挑选最佳模型，而在于在模型之上构建一个学习循环，让人力资本与代币资本在其中复合增长。你可以外包一项任务，甚至一个岗位，但永远无法外包自己的学习。企业的未来，在于能否将这种学习能力在人与AI之间实现复合增长。这需要全新的架构方法，让每个企业都能构建随时间不断进化的智能体系统，同时牢牢掌控自己的知识产权。一家公司应当能够随时更换'通才型'模型，而不会丢失其学习系统中积累的'资深专家'级知识。"我们都在共同探索AI未来的正确架构。但显而易见的是，绝大部分力量与价值，都将流向那些能最有效利用任何AI系统来挖掘自身信息的地方。这也正是应用层AI在未来几年将获得巨大价值的原因所在。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: Having been through many frontier model launch reviews, I have empathy for everyone involved. Launching an LLM isn't like shipping traditional software - you're making a decision about a black box with effectively infinite use cases and infinite failure modes. The tradeoffs are hard - every increase in capability expands the space of both valuable use cases and potential misuse. As a lab, you build extensive evals, you red-team, you iterate on the model. You debate tradeoffs across candidate checkpoints before choosing the best one to launch. Then early-access partners still uncover behaviors you didn't anticipate. You can never be 100% certain you've understood a frontier model. You focus on reducing the uncertainty enough to launch. As frontier models become smarter across the industry, that decision will get harder - for labs and regulators.
remarks.translation: 经历过多次前沿模型发布评审，我对所有参与者都深表理解。发布大语言模型不同于交付传统软件——你是在对一个拥有近乎无限使用场景和无限故障模式的黑箱做出决策。权衡取舍异常艰难——每一次能力提升，既拓展了有价值的应用场景，也扩大了潜在滥用的空间。作为实验室，你需要构建全面的评估体系，开展红队测试，对模型进行迭代优化。在多个候选检查点之间反复权衡利弊，最终选定最佳版本发布。即便如此，早期体验合作伙伴仍会发现你未曾预料的行为模式。你永远无法100%确定自己已完全理解一个前沿模型。你的重点是将不确定性降低到足以发布的程度。随着整个行业的前沿模型日益智能，这个决策对实验室和监管机构而言都将变得更加艰难。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text: ## The Future Codebase After the PR dies, after the Code Review dies, i am seriously wondering if Git needs to die next. roughly 20-40% of code spend is just managing and updating merge conflicts. necessary evil? or legacy "horseless carriage"? cargo culting the past? we don't do line by line merge conflicts when we collaborate with human colleagues - instead we chat, suggest edits, do side comments, and an owner ships it. btw we also don't do CI/CD even collaborating on documents with serious legal/financial implications. maybe the future codebase looks more like a Notion or Linear database than .git objects. It will be less efficient, but more scalable. exactly the Salty Lesson.
remarks.translation: 在 PR 消亡之后，在代码审查消亡之后，我真的很怀疑 Git 是否也该是下一个被淘汰的。大约 20-40% 的编码时间都花在了管理和更新合并冲突上。这是必要的恶？还是遗留的“无马马车”？对过去的盲目崇拜？当我们与人类同事协作时，我们不会逐行处理合并冲突——而是聊天、建议编辑、做旁注，然后由负责人发布。顺便说一句，即使在处理具有重大法律/财务影响的文档时，我们也不使用 CI/CD。也许未来的代码库看起来更像一个 Notion 或 Linear 数据库，而不是 .git 对象。它的效率会降低，但可扩展性更强。这正是那个苦涩的教训。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.text: Everyone thinks AI coding tools set founders free. Watch what people actually build with them: rules, approvals, process, layers. The same cage, assembled faster. The tool that can scaffold anything in an afternoon will scaffold your bureaucracy in an afternoon too. Speed of construction is speed of calcification. Build the thing that lets you create new things: experiences that didn’t happen before.
remarks.translation: 人人都以为AI编程工具能让创始人获得自由。看看人们实际用它搭建了什么：规则、审批、流程、层级。同样的牢笼，只是组装得更快。那个能在一下午搭出任何东西的工具，也会在一下午搭出你的官僚体系。建造的速度，就是僵化的速度。去打造那个能让你创造新事物的东西：那些从未发生过的体验。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text: ## On Loopcraft One might argue the entire game of the next century is to be able to stack loops as effectively as possible. In the early days of each phase, it will be valuable to know when to go **DOWN** a loop when things go wrong (for reliability)… but it will probably be more valuable to know how to go **UP** a loop as models improve (for leverage). If you don’t figure out how to do this, don’t be salty when you lose to those that do.
remarks.translation:
## 论循环工艺
或许有人会说，下个世纪整场游戏的核心，就是尽可能高效地堆叠循环。在每个阶段的早期，懂得在出问题时如何**向下**进入循环（为了可靠性）会很有价值……但随着模型不断改进，懂得如何**向上**进入循环（为了杠杆效应）可能更有价值。如果你没搞明白怎么做到这一点，就别在输给那些做到的人时酸溜溜的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: At Box, we just surveyed 1,640 IT leaders across the US, Japan, and Europe about agentic AI adoption. Many standout findings, but a big one was that the companies that adopted AI the most are planning to grow headcount the most. Obviously lots of ways you can read that data and variables mixed in, but it’s actually quite intuitive that the companies that become most productive want to (and are able to) reinvest back into the business to keep getting the gains going. The narrative of jobs being wiped out assumes that companies will take a fixed approach to what they want to be able for work on. What’s happening in practice is it’s causing companies to want to light up more engineering projects, sell to more customers, automate more processes to give time back, and more. That all leads to more work to be done by people.
remarks.translation: Box 近期对美、日、欧三地 1,640 名 IT 领导者进行了关于智能体 AI 采纳情况的调研。诸多发现引人注目，其中一项重大发现是：AI 应用最深入的企业，其人员扩编计划也最为积极。显然，解读这些数据的方式和混杂变量有很多，但一个很直观的逻辑是——那些实现最高生产力的企业，往往希望（也有能力）将收益重新投入到业务中，以持续推动增长。所谓“岗位消亡”的叙事，是假设企业对其工作范畴采取固定不变的策略。而现实情况是，AI 正促使企业启动更多工程项目、拓展更多客户、自动化更多流程以释放时间，诸如此类。这一切，最终都指向了更多需要人力完成的工作。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Lots of evidence of huge jumps in capability for Fable across coding (and related) tasks. It’s also a major jump in accuracy and success in complex knowledge work tasks. In our Box AI Complex Work Eval, we tested the model against Opus 4.8 and saw huge boosts across almost every industry. For our eval we give the Box AI Agent, using Fable, a set of hard real world knowledge work problems that deal with enterprise documents. Then score how the agent performs the tasks. The main differentiators for Fable vs Opus 4.8 is that it doesn't take shortcuts on complex reasoning, it gets multi-step calculations right, and it's significantly more consistent across runs. We saw the biggest leaps in Media & Entertainment (78% vs 61%), Technology (81% vs 73%), Financial Services (89% vs 83%), and Healthcare (66% vs 60%). Here are some specific examples: * Legal M&A due diligence: On a task reviewing NDA terms against a semiconductor company's contracting policy, Fable correctly identified that a joint-ownership clause violates exclusivity requirements while a liability cap is permitted under a Super Cap exception. Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 78%. * Healthcare: On a clinical radiology error audit across 12 reports, Fable precisely categorized each error by severity grade and correctly concluded no Grade 3 errors existed. Opus prematurely escalated a case to "major error requiring immediate departmental review" when the evidence didn't support it — Fable 63% vs Opus 41%. * Media & Entertainment: On a genre profitability projection task, Fable correctly recognized that a 20% Argentine tax deduction was already embedded in the source spreadsheet figures and didn't double-apply it. Opus applied it again on top — a compounding error across 4 genre calculations that took its score negative on the task vs Fable's 74%. * Retail analytics: On a task analyzing high-growth product articles against an investment benchmark, Fable correctly computed each article's growth rate individually and identified that only 2 of 5 exceeded the threshold. Opus confused "high growth relative to average" with "above the benchmark" — scoring 61% vs Fable's 94%. * Financial Services: On a 5-year debt facility projection, Fable correctly applied interest to opening balances and used the right capex figure. Opus applied interest to the total facility amount and computed tax from the wrong base — two compounding errors. Fable scored 83% vs Opus's 62%. * Technology: On a SaaS feature valuation requiring computation of a Feature Value Index across multiple regions, Fable applied the formula correctly and got exact values for the markets. Opus got the arithmetic wrong on multiple criteria — Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 74%. Overall, huge step change in complex analysis, work that requires analytical reasoning, and deep domain understanding. Fable will be available shortly in the Box AI Studio for customers to build agents with.
remarks.translation: 在编程（及相关）任务中，有大量证据表明 Fable 的能力实现了巨大飞跃。在复杂知识工作任务的准确性和成功率方面，它同样取得了重大进步。在我们的 Box AI 复杂工作评估中，我们将该模型与 Opus 4.8 进行了对比测试，发现几乎每个行业都有显著提升。在评估中，我们让使用 Fable 的 Box AI 智能体处理一系列涉及企业文档的真实高难度知识工作问题，然后对其任务执行表现进行评分。Fable 与 Opus 4.8 的主要区别在于：它不会在复杂推理上走捷径，能正确处理多步骤计算，并且在不同运行中的表现一致性显著更高。我们观察到提升最大的领域包括媒体与娱乐（78% 对 61%）、科技（81% 对 73%）、金融服务（89% 对 83%）以及医疗健康（66% 对 60%）。以下是一些具体示例：* 法律并购尽职调查：在一项对照某半导体公司合同政策审查保密协议条款的任务中，Fable 正确识别出共同所有权条款违反了排他性要求，而责任上限在超级上限例外情况下是允许的。Fable 得分为 100%，Opus 为 78%。* 医疗健康：在一项针对 12 份报告的临床放射学错误审计中，Fable 精确地按严重等级对每个错误进行了分类，并正确得出不存在 3 级错误的结论。Opus 则在证据不支持的情况下，过早将一个案例升级为“需要立即进行部门审查的重大错误”——Fable 得分为 63%，Opus 为 41%。* 媒体与娱乐：在一项类型片盈利预测任务中，Fable 正确识别出 20% 的阿根廷税收减免已包含在源表格数据中，因此没有重复计算。Opus 则在此基础上再次应用了该减免——这一复合错误贯穿了 4 项类型片计算，导致其任务得分为负，而 Fable 得分为 74%。* 零售分析：在一项对照投资基准分析高增长产品文章的任务中，Fable 正确计算了每篇文章的独立增长率，并识别出 5 篇中仅有 2 篇超过了阈值。Opus 混淆了“相对于平均水平的高增长”与“高于基准”——得分为 61%，而 Fable 为 94%。* 金融服务：在一项五年期债务融资预测中，Fable 正确地将利息应用于期初余额，并使用了正确的资本支出数据。Opus 则将利息应用于融资总额，并基于错误的基础计算了税款——造成了两项复合错误。Fable 得分为 83%，Opus 为 62%。* 科技：在一项需要跨多个区域计算功能价值指数的 SaaS 功能估值任务中，Fable 正确应用了公式，并得出了各市场的精确数值。Opus 在多个标准的计算上出现错误——Fable 得分为 100%，Opus 为 74%。总体而言，在复杂分析、需要分析推理的工作以及深度领域理解方面，这是一次巨大的阶跃变化。Fable 即将在 Box AI Studio 中上线，供客户构建智能体使用。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text: People should build agents/skills for their cross-functional teams. For example, if a design team builds a design agent/skill for the marketing team (trained on all of the brand's guidelines and design patterns), then the marketing team can produce more on-brand assets without having to bug designers every time But the marketing team couldn't have built this on their own; it takes the designer with their expertise, context, and knowledge Same thing applies to every pair of teams that work closely with each other, often rely on each other, and complain about each other's limited bandwidth Building agents for your cross-functional teams ensures each team can be more self-sufficient, and moves us to a direction where teams can be organized by "loops" rather than "functions"
remarks.translation: 人们应该为自己的跨职能团队构建智能体/技能。举个例子，如果设计团队为营销团队打造一个设计智能体/技能（基于品牌所有指南和设计模式进行训练），那么营销团队就能产出更多符合品牌调性的素材，而不必每次都去打扰设计师。但营销团队无法独自完成这项工作；这需要设计师凭借其专业知识、背景理解和经验积累来实现。同样的道理适用于任何紧密协作、相互依赖，却又常抱怨对方带宽有限的团队组合。为跨职能团队构建智能体，能确保每个团队更加自给自足，并推动我们朝着以"循环"而非"职能"来组织团队的方向发展。

remarks.name: Matt Turck
remarks.role: VC at FirstMarkCap. Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
remarks.text: 2026 is a BRUTAL grind in VC. You start in Davos, freeze in Aspen, hit Upfront, survive Milken, then it’s straight to Paris for the French Open. Briefly back in NYC for the Knicks. Then, total blur: SuperReturn in Berlin, Founders Forum in London, then back stateside for the World Cup, back to Paris for Raise AI, Idaho for Sun Valley, quick respite in Mykonos, then the Goldman tech gauntlet, Slush in Finland, NeurIPS in freakin’ Sydney… and *boom*, a productive year of thought leadership and adding value is over, and you’re a wreck.
remarks.translation: 2026年对风投行业而言是一场残酷的马拉松。从达沃斯启程，在阿斯彭冻得发抖，赶赴Upfront大会，熬过米尔肯峰会，紧接着直奔巴黎看法国网球公开赛。短暂回纽约观战尼克斯队比赛后，便是彻底混乱的行程：柏林超级回报峰会、伦敦创始人论坛，再飞回美国看世界杯，折返巴黎参加Raise AI大会，转战爱达荷州太阳谷，在米科诺斯岛稍作喘息，接着是高盛科技大考、芬兰Slush大会、远赴悉尼的NeurIPS会议……然后"砰"的一声，这一年充满思想领导力与价值创造的日子结束了，而你已精疲力竭。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: Right from the early days of Gemini, enterprises would get the quality/cost tradeoff wrong. They’d often start with the smallest, cheapest model. The rule of thumb we gave customers: Replacing a traditional ML model with an LLM - start small, cos you already know what good looks like. Building something new - start with the most capable model. Think magically. Figure out what’s actually possible first. Once they had a high-quality working application, we’d help them move to a smaller model while maintaining quality.
remarks.translation: 早在 Gemini 问世之初，企业就常常在质量与成本的权衡上出错。它们往往一开始就选择最小、最便宜的模型。我们给客户的经验法则是：如果用大语言模型替代传统机器学习模型，就从小的开始——因为你已经知道理想效果是什么样。如果是构建全新应用，则从能力最强的模型入手。要大胆想象，先弄清楚真正能实现什么。一旦他们有了高质量的可运行应用，我们就会帮他们在保持质量的前提下迁移到更小的模型。

remarks.text: The barrier for non-technical people using coding agents was never the interface. Chatting is the easiest UI ever invented The barrier is that they don't know what to ask for. The blank chat box assumes you already know what's possible. Most people don't. When I tried Town, I was impressed by its onboarding process where the agent proactively suggested workflows & things it can take off my plate, rather than waiting for me to give instructions
remarks.translation: 非技术人员使用编程助手的障碍从来不是界面。聊天是人类发明的最简单的交互方式。真正的障碍在于他们不知道要提出什么需求。空白的聊天框默认你已经知道什么是可行的，但大多数人并不清楚。当我试用Town时，其引导流程让我印象深刻：智能体会主动建议工作流程和它能替我分担的事务，而不是被动等待我下达指令。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: This is a critical post to read if you’re building an applied AI company right now. “An application earns its place in the untrainable corner by doing unglamorous work: arranging a company's private reality so a model can act on it, handing the model the tools to act, working with the customer to change the reality of its workforce. A company that brings the translation is tough to copy – and the translation never ends. Integration and maintenance run as long as the relationship does, won by teams that put domain-specialized engineers and tools next to the customer.” There’s still an insanely large gulf between model capabilities and what it takes to apply them to specific corporate workflows. Some of that is technology that needs to be built, a lot is access to (and formatting of) the right data to work with, and a ton more is on the change management and specific implementation work (FDEs, etc.) it takes to make AI work in any specific corporate setting. 2 things can be very true at once: frontier models and labs will continue to grow an incredible amount, and there will be a vast ecosystem of software and services companies that emerge to bring the power of these models to real enterprises. This makes room for new infrastructure provides, applied AI companies in every vertical, new versions of system integrators, and more players. Incredibly exciting time on all fronts.
remarks.translation: 如果你正在创办一家应用型人工智能公司，这篇文章至关重要。“一款应用之所以能在‘无法训练的角落’占据一席之地，靠的是那些不起眼的工作：梳理企业的内部现实，让模型能够据此行动；为模型提供行动所需的工具；与客户合作，改变其员工队伍的现实。能够完成这种‘转译’的公司很难被复制——而这种转译永无止境。集成和维护会随着客户关系的持续而一直进行，只有那些将领域专业工程师和工具部署在客户身边的团队，才能赢得这场持久战。”模型能力与将其应用于特定企业工作流程所需的条件之间，仍然存在着巨大的鸿沟。其中一部分是需要构建的技术，很大一部分是获取（并格式化）正确的数据以供使用，还有更多则涉及变更管理和具体的实施工作（如前端工程师等），这些都是在任何特定企业环境中让人工智能发挥作用所必需的。2件事可以同时成立：前沿模型和实验室将继续取得惊人的增长，同时也会涌现出庞大的软件和服务公司生态系统，将这些模型的力量带给真正的企业。这为新基础设施提供商、各个垂直领域的应用型人工智能公司、新一代系统集成商以及更多参与者创造了空间。各个方面都处于一个令人无比振奋的时代。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews to help busy people become AI builders
remarks.text: Btw this is the prompt I used: Build F-Zero: futuristic anti-gravity racer with pseudo-3D track (raycasting or mode-7 style scaling), 3 AI opponents, boost meter that drains health, speed 400-800 km/h with visible sense of speed via track warping and scrolling ground pattern, arrow keys to steer, shift to boost, 3 laps on a looping track with checkpoints. Style it neon cyberpunk (dark sky, glowing track edges, chromatic aberration on boost), show HUD with position/lap/speed/health, and make the ship bank into turns. Prioritize feel over realism - the speed sensation is the whole game.
remarks.translation: 顺便说一下，这是我用的提示词：打造《F-Zero》风格游戏——未来感反重力赛车，采用伪3D赛道（光线投射或Mode-7式缩放），3名AI对手，加速计量表消耗生命值，速度400-800 km/h，通过赛道扭曲和滚动地面纹理营造明显速度感，方向键操控，Shift键加速，在环形赛道上跑3圈并设有检查点。风格采用霓虹赛博朋克（暗黑天空、发光赛道边缘、加速时产生色差效果），显示HUD界面，包含排名/圈数/速度/生命值，飞船在转弯时倾斜。优先追求速度感而非真实感——速度体验就是整个游戏的核心。

remarks.name: Alex Albert
remarks.role: Research AnthropicAI. Opinions are my own!
remarks.text: We've reset usage limits across our products! For those just starting to test Fable, here's four tips for using it more effectively: 1. Give it bigger, more ambitious tasks than what previous models could handle. 2. Use xhigh/high effort as your default for best performance, med for faster interactive sessions. 3. Rework your skills and CLAUDE.mds. Instructions written for prior models anchor Fable to stale patterns, let it use its own judgment first. 4. Move from providing tasks to providing objectives. Describe what done looks like and how to verify it, then let Fable find the path (/loop and /goal are built for this)
remarks.translation: 我们已重置所有产品的使用限额！对于刚开始测试 Fable 的用户，这里有四条更高效使用的建议：1. 交给它比以往模型能处理的更大、更具挑战性的任务。2. 将 xhigh/high 努力级别设为默认以获得最佳性能，med 则用于更快的交互会话。3. 重新调整你的技能和 CLAUDE.mds。为先前模型编写的指令会让 Fable 固守旧模式，先让它自行判断。4. 从提供任务转向提供目标。描述完成后的状态及验证方式，然后让 Fable 自行探索路径（/loop 和 /goal 正是为此设计）。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text: We talk a lot about how important it is to set up self-verification loops. Especially in the age of powerful models that can run for long periods of time, self-verification is a key ingredient that enables the model to run for much longer, delivering a result that is closer to what you intended, so you can do more without having to constantly check in on Claude as it works. @delba_oliveira gives a great breakdown of what that looks like and why it matters
remarks.translation: 我们经常强调建立自我验证循环的重要性。尤其是在强大模型能够长时间运行的当下，自我验证是让模型持续运作更久、产出更贴近你预期结果的关键要素——这样你就能在无需频繁检查Claude工作状态的情况下完成更多任务。@delba_oliveira 对此进行了精彩解析，阐述了其具体形态与重要意义。

remarks.name: Andrej Karpathy
remarks.role: I like training large deep neural nets.
remarks.text: This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time. I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!
remarks.translation: 这是一个超级激动人心的发布——Claude Fable 5 与 Mythos 共享相同的底层模型，但增加了额外的安全防护。基准测试表现优异，在所有方面都以明显优势达到顶尖水平，但我想补充的是，从*定性*角度来看，这也是一次值得大版本号升级的飞跃式进步（在我看来，其重要性与去年11月 Claude 4.5 的发布相当），尤其在处理极难问题的长时间解题环节中表现突出。你可以给它布置比以往更具雄心的任务，模型能“领会”意图并直接推进，而且从未有过如此强烈的冲动让人完全不想再看代码（但千万别在生产环境中这么做！）。模型仍有一些人们会遇到的小毛病，且安全防护在发布初期配置得有些过于敏感，希望后续能逐步调优。随着工作软件越来越能一键生成，我感受到许多变化正在发生。杰文斯悖论开始显现，我感觉到自己对软件的需求正在大幅增长。你可以提出任何要求——解释器、可视化工具、仪表盘、定制的一次性应用（例如一个专为你项目高度定制的完整 wandb），你可以将测试套件效率提升 10X，自动优化代码，运行带有自定义 HTML 结果展示的大型研究项目，无所不能！“解放你的思想”（《黑客帝国》梗）。非常期待看到大家构建出的一切！

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Great post. So much about model performance is a function of how much compute you’re doing at inference time. This means compute-normalized benchmarks is the only logical path forward. And yet, the challenge is it’s a lot harder than it seems given it’s subjective how much compute to apply, which means models behave differently at different thresholds (simplistically, model X’s min thinking may beat model Y’s min thinking, but be reversed at high), and there are a near infinite set of thresholds you could choose to set. But either way, moving more in this direction would be great for better understanding AI progress.
remarks.translation: 精彩观点。模型性能在很大程度上取决于推理时的计算量投入。这意味着计算归一化基准测试是唯一合乎逻辑的发展方向。然而挑战在于，实际操作远比想象中困难——因为投入多少计算量存在主观性，导致模型在不同阈值下表现各异（简单来说，模型X的最低思考量可能优于模型Y的最低思考量，但在高计算量时情况可能逆转），而且可供选择的阈值组合近乎无限。但无论如何，朝着这个方向深入探索，都将极大促进我们对AI进展的理解。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: There’s no amount of intelligence that can get packed into AI models that replaces the need for context. For any sufficiently general purpose AI, you will always have to guide it in the direction you want as it has an infinite range of directions it can go in. As long as the same model is used by a lawyer, an engineer, a financial analyst, or a healthcare professional, and as long as you’re trying to do anything uniquely differentiated or specific, then instructions, domain context, and proprietary data will always need to get into the context window for the model to be useful. This is partly why AI automation doesn’t come for free, and why there’s still a wide spectrum of who’s getting the largest gains from AI and who’s not. You have to put in real work, and you get real value on the other end. This is one of the advantages that applied AI will also have in the market. Any layer of abstraction above just the raw intelligence that can meaningfully get you off to the races faster will likely continue to be valuable.
remarks.translation: 无论AI模型被赋予多少智能，都无法取代上下文的重要性。对于任何足够通用的AI，你始终需要引导它朝着你期望的方向发展，因为它可以走向无限多的方向。只要同一个模型被律师、工程师、金融分析师或医疗专业人员使用，并且你试图完成任何独特或特定的事情，那么指令、领域上下文和专有数据就必须进入上下文窗口，模型才能发挥作用。这就是为什么AI自动化并非免费，也是为什么在从AI中获得最大收益的人群中，仍然存在巨大差异的原因。你必须付出真正的努力，才能获得真正的价值。这也是应用型AI在市场上将拥有的优势之一。任何在原始智能之上，能够让你更快起步的有意义的抽象层，都可能继续具有价值。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: The numbers may be a bit extreme here, but unquestionably use-cases have to stratify in the next year or two between model families. We’ll see a split between frontier intelligence for high end tasks and work, and much cheaper models for high volume workloads that can sufficiently be peeled off to cheaper models. Frontier will still be far bigger than today because the use-cases will demand it, but the low-end will get quite a bit larger as well. The big update here is that the layer that can efficiently route the workload to the right model will then become increasingly valuable since that becomes one of the new hard problems in AI agents. Agent orchestration that can cost optimize while still performing the task successfully will be in a strong position.
remarks.translation: 这里的数字或许有些极端，但毫无疑问，在未来一两年内，不同模型系列之间的应用场景必将分层。我们将看到一种分化：面向高端任务和工作场景的前沿智能模型，以及针对海量工作负载、可充分剥离至低成本模型的廉价版本。前沿模型的规模仍将远超今日，因为应用场景会提出更高要求，但低端模型的市场也将大幅扩张。这里的关键更新在于，能够高效将工作负载路由至合适模型的那一层，将变得愈发珍贵，因为这将成为AI智能体领域新的难题之一。那些既能成功完成任务，又能实现成本优化的智能体编排系统，将占据强势地位。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text: Seeing a number of benchmarks showing Opus is the best model for long-running work. Five tips for running Opus autonomously for hours/days: 1. Use auto mode for permissions, so Claude doesn’t ask for approval 2. Use dynamic workflows, to have Claude orchestrate hundreds/thousands of agents to get a task done 3. Use /goal or /loop, to nudge Claude to keep going until it’s done 4. Use Claude Code in the cloud, so you can close your laptop (easiest way is the desktop or mobile app) 5. Make sure Claude has a way to self-verify its work end to end: Claude in Chrome browser extension for web, iOS/Android sim MCP for mobile, a way to start the full web server or service for backend work
remarks.translation:
看到多项基准测试显示，Opus 是长时间运行任务的最佳模型。以下是让 Opus 自主运行数小时/数天的五个技巧：  
1. 使用自动模式处理权限，这样 Claude 就不会请求批准  
2. 使用动态工作流，让 Claude 协调数百/数千个代理来完成任务  
3. 使用 /goal 或 /loop，推动 Claude 持续运行直至完成  
4. 在云端使用 Claude Code，这样你可以合上笔记本电脑（最简单的方式是通过桌面或移动应用）  
5. 确保 Claude 有办法端到端地自我验证其工作：通过 Chrome 浏览器扩展让 Claude 处理网页端，通过 iOS/Android 模拟 MCP 处理移动端，通过启动完整 Web 服务器或服务处理后端工作

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: This is what the market got wrong about AI eating enterprise software. Building good software in the past was very hard. Yes, AI has made that a bit easier, though it’s still hard to build something that’s got good taste, differentiated, high quality, secure, and so on. But nevertheless, that’s only one component of building a platform that enterprises rely on. The plurality of costs in most enterprise software companies is actually on GTM, because at scale most enterprise software categories are tough to break into and need a heavy amount of consultative selling and support for implementation and integration of solutions. AI hasn’t reduced the need for that, and in many cases requires it even more now, as landscapes get even more busy and complicated for buyers to navigate through. If you make one thing cheaper and more abundant (development of software) then the new problem of discoverability and market differentiation (GTM) becomes the hardest part.
remarks.translation: 这就是市场对AI吞噬企业软件的错误认知。过去打造优质软件极其困难。诚然，AI让这个过程变得稍微容易了些，但要构建出品味出众、差异化显著、品质卓越且安全可靠的软件，依然充满挑战。不过，这仅仅是打造企业级平台的一个环节。大多数企业软件公司的成本大头其实集中在市场推广上，因为规模化后，多数企业软件品类都难以突破，需要大量的咨询式销售，以及解决方案实施与集成的支持。AI并未减少这方面的需求，在许多情况下反而使其更为迫切——随着市场格局愈发纷繁复杂，采购方需要更专业的引导。当某个环节（软件开发）变得更廉价、更充裕时，新的难题——可发现性与市场差异化（市场推广）——就成了最难攻克的部分。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text: A common misconception is that training data is low skill, grunt work - scan some notebooks, mine the internet, create labeled samples. The data required to advance the model frontier is the opposite. Labs need training data for high-economic-value tasks. And most of these tasks outside of SWE have little documentation - it is complex, domain-specific knowledge built over the years, spanning legacy tools that don’t talk to each other. That's why we have SWE agents and not knowledge work agents yet. The companies creating this training data, such as Mercor, are doing extremely high-leverage, high-skill work. Critical to moving AI forward. And deeply underappreciated.
remarks.translation: 一种常见的误解是，认为训练数据工作技术含量低、纯属苦力活——无非是扫描些笔记本、从互联网上挖掘信息、创建标注样本。但推动模型前沿发展所需的数据恰恰相反。实验室需要的是针对高经济价值任务的训练数据。而在软件工程之外，这类任务大多缺乏文档记录——它们涉及复杂、特定领域的知识，是多年积累而成，横跨彼此不兼容的遗留工具。这就是为什么我们目前只有软件工程智能体，而尚未出现知识工作智能体的原因。像Mercor这样创建此类训练数据的公司，正在从事极具杠杆效应、高技术含量的工作。这对推动人工智能发展至关重要，却远未得到应有的重视。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Token costs are becoming one of the hottest topics for any enterprise I talk with right now. It’s very bullish for AI in general because it means these systems are being used at a scale that wasn’t contemplated before. It also gives way to another form of differentiation that will emerge for the applied AI layer, which is model routing. As tokens take on a significant amount of the cost of any given workflow, then companies will inevitably want to ensure that their dollars go into the most efficient use of tokens for the particular job at hand. Frontier intelligence will always be relevant at the high end of tasks, like coding, legal and financial analysis, healthcare, and more. And dollars spent here will only go up over time. But, equally, you can peel off individual tasks to lower cost models (whether they’re from open weights vendors or the major labs) and deliver a more efficient end outcome. To do this effectively, the applied AI layer needs to understand the workflows in their domain better than anyone else, and be able to mix and match models to different jobs. If you’re doing document extraction, you need to know which models perform better or worse for any given document type. If you’re legal analysis, you want to know which models perform various types of tasks best. And so on. This will become one of the bigger differentiation points over time. The companies with the best evals, the best ability to route the workloads, and those that have business models directly aligned to customers financial goals, will be in a great position.
remarks.translation: 代币成本正迅速成为我目前接触的所有企业最关注的话题之一。这对人工智能整体而言极为利好，因为它意味着这些系统的应用规模已超出此前预期。这也为应用型人工智能层开辟了另一种差异化竞争方向——模型路由。随着代币在任何工作流成本中占据显著比重，企业必然希望确保资金能最高效地用于特定任务的代币使用。前沿智能在编程、法律金融分析、医疗等高端任务领域始终不可或缺，相关投入只会持续增长。但同样地，你可以将单项任务剥离给成本更低的模型（无论是来自开放权重供应商还是主流实验室），从而获得更高效的最终成果。要实现这一目标，应用型人工智能层需要比任何人都更透彻地理解其领域内的工作流，并能针对不同任务灵活组合模型。若从事文档提取，你需要掌握各类文档类型中哪些模型表现更优或更差；若从事法律分析，则需了解哪些模型最擅长处理特定类型的任务，诸如此类。这将成为未来重要的差异化竞争点。那些拥有最佳评估体系、最强工作负载路由能力，且商业模式与客户财务目标高度契合的企业，将占据显著优势。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text: a smarter alternative to "always use plan mode": always frame your task as a question, so that the model is invited to push back and rate the quality of the idea/suggest alternatives, rather than blindly execute what you SAID to do (which is often not precisely what you MEANT) literally just appending "?" to the end of your prompt often does it
remarks.translation: 比“始终使用计划模式”更聪明的做法是：始终将你的任务表述为一个问题，这样模型就会被引导去提出异议、评估想法的质量或建议替代方案，而不是盲目执行你所说的内容（这往往并非你真正想表达的意思）。实际上，只需在提示词末尾加上一个问号“？”，通常就能达到效果。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text: Coding is basically the pinnacle of what you could reasonably automate with AI, and yet we still need human engineers to oversee agents for them to be effective. The AI models are trained on an incredible amount of sophisticated code. The users are highly technical and can use the latest tools quickly. The work is “verifiable” because you can test an app. The outcomes are often removed from the quality of the code (you can have sloppy code but the app can still work). And the context for the agent is often already digitized and sitting in the codebase. That’s an incredible amount of benefits that AI coding agents get to work with. Some of those apply to knowledge work, but most don’t in areas where the work needs to be fully reviewed to be useful, or where data isn’t as abundantly digitized. This makes the job for agents in knowledge work more complicated. So if with all of that, engineers still remain in very high demand, the risks are going to be less than what’s perceived for other areas of knowledge work. Agents will let people do far more than they did before, but the people don’t go away.
remarks.translation: 编程可以说是AI能够合理自动化的巅峰领域，然而我们仍然需要人类工程师监督智能体，才能让它们真正发挥作用。AI模型在海量复杂代码上接受训练，用户群体技术能力极强，能快速掌握最新工具。这项工作具有"可验证性"——因为你可以直接测试应用程序。成果质量往往与代码本身优劣脱钩（即便代码写得粗糙，应用仍能正常运行）。而智能体所需的上下文信息通常已经数字化并存在于代码库中。这些是AI编程智能体得天独厚的优势。其中部分优势也适用于知识型工作，但在需要全面审核才能产生价值、或数据数字化程度不高的领域，大多数优势并不存在。这使得知识型工作中的智能体面临更复杂的挑战。因此，即便在具备所有优势的编程领域，工程师需求依然旺盛，其他知识型工作领域的风险其实比人们想象的要小。智能体将让人们实现远超以往的产出，但人类本身并不会被取代。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text: How to build AI skills that check their own work and improve over time: 1. Give it context Ask AI: "Create a skill for this [repeated task]. Here are examples of good output so it knows what good looks like." 2. Make it easy to trigger “Write a clear skill description using this pattern: Use when the user wants to [do this].” 3. Add evals "Create an evals md with 10 pass/fail checks for common errors in the skill's output." 4. Add memory "Create a memory md to capture one-sentence learnings from past chats using this skill." 5. Build a skill to edit skills "Create a skill that cleans up other skills by removing duplicate or stale instructions, vague rules, and AI slop." 📌 Watch my full walkthrough here: https://t.co/u434ytNSZd
remarks.translation:
如何构建能自我检查并持续优化的AI技能：  
1. 赋予上下文  
向AI提问：“为这项[重复性任务]创建技能。以下是优质输出范例，让它明确优秀的标准。”  

2. 简化触发方式  
“用以下模式编写清晰的技能描述：当用户需要[执行此操作]时启用。”  

3. 添加评估机制  
“创建一份评估文档，包含10项针对技能输出常见错误的通过/失败检查项。”  

4. 融入记忆功能  
“创建记忆文档，用于记录过往对话中使用该技能时提炼的单句经验。”  

5. 构建技能编辑工具  
“创建一项技能，用于清理其他技能中的重复或过时指令、模糊规则及AI冗余内容。”  

📌 点击此处观看完整实操演示：https://t.co/u434ytNSZd

podcasts.name: Training Data (Sequoia)
podcasts.title: 工厂的Matan Grinberg：即将到来的“黑灯工厂”，软件将自行构建
podcasts.originalTitle: Factory's Matan Grinberg: The Coming ‘Dark Factory’ Where Software Builds Itself
podcasts.excerpt: 工厂于2023年4月开始打造全自主编程智能体，比企业真正准备好早了整整两年。马坦·格林伯格如今坦言，这无异于判断失误。这位Factory联合创始人兼CEO讲述了公司如何熬过那段"沙漠跋涉期"，包括在产品未能让开发者痴迷时，毅然将几乎全部收入返还给客户。马坦提出了一个反常规的技术观点：模型无关的集成框架，优于OpenAI和Anthropic等实验室偏好的模型与框架协同设计，因为让集成框架接触多种模型，能避免其过度适配任何单一模型。他认为，像GLM这样的开放权重模型，只需保持比前沿技术落后一代，就能以极低的成本占据大部分token使用量；而CIO们很快会像评估人力投入一样，为每个新增token的使用找到合理依据。展望未来，他预测90%的编程工作将……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: Factory started building fully autonomous coding agents in April 2023, two years before enterprises were ready. Matan Grinberg now says this is indistinguishable from being wrong. The Factory co-founder and CEO explains how the company survived its "journey in the desert," including the decision to hand nearly all of its revenue back to customers when the product wasn't making developers obsessed. Matan makes the contrarian technical case that a model-agnostic harness beats the model-and-harness co-design that labs like OpenAI and Anthropic favor, because exposing a harness to many models keeps it from overfitting to any single one. He argues open-weight models like GLM will capture the majority of tokens by staying one generation behind the frontier at a fraction of the cost, and that CIOs will soon justify every incremental token the way they justify headcount. Looking ahead, he predicts 90% of coding to…

podcasts.title: 第90集：人工智能先驱尤尔根·施密德胡伯谈当今AI现状
podcasts.originalTitle: Ep 90: AI Pioneer Jürgen Schmidhuber on the State of AI Today
podcasts.excerpt: 尤尔根·施密德胡伯博士，这位享誉全球的科学家与人工智能研究者，被公认为该领域的先驱之一。他通过其实验室的工作，孕育了当今Transformer架构、长短期记忆网络（LSTM）以及递归自我改进等关键理念。他认为，真正的通用人工智能（AGI）仍受限于物理硬件瓶颈；随着开源技术紧追封闭实验室的步伐，当前对AI数据中心的投资热潮即将迎来修正；而通往通用智能的路径，在于人工好奇心与自主生成的实验，而非依赖互联网数据。在结尾部分，他重新审视了主流AI安全论点，并描绘了一幅宏大愿景：自我复制的机器人社会终将殖民整个太阳系。 (0:00) 引言 (1:24) 超人类AI还有多远？ (2:27) ChatGPT为何未令他惊讶 (3:21) 递归自我改进之路 (9:01) AI会迎来爆发式飞跃吗…
podcasts.originalExcerpt: Dr. Jürgen Schmidhuber, a renowned scientist and AI researcher widely regarded as one of the pioneers in the field, originated key ideas behind today's transformers, LSTMs, and recursive self-improvement through his lab's work. He argues that true AGI remains bottlenecked by physical hardware, that today's AI data center investments are headed for a correction as open-source keeps pace with closed labs, and that the path to general intelligence runs through artificial curiosity and self-generated experimentation rather than internet data. He closes by reconsidering mainstream AI safety arguments and offers a sweeping vision of self-replicating robot societies eventually colonizing the solar system. (0:00) Intro (1:24) How Close Is Superhuman AI? (2:27) Why ChatGPT Didn't Surprise Him (3:21) The Path to Recursive Self-Improvement (9:01) Will AI Takeoff…

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: 与DoorDash联合创始人Andy Fang和Stanley Tang共同打造自主配送体验
podcasts.originalTitle: Building an Autonomous Delivery Experience with DoorDash Co-Founders Andy Fang and Stanley Tang
podcasts.excerpt: DoorDash 不仅仅是一家配送公司。从创立之初，联合创始人 Andy Fang 和 Stanley Tang 就将其作为一家机器人与自动驾驶公司来运营。Andy 和 Stanley 与 Sarah Guo 一起探讨了自动驾驶技术和人工智能如何重塑消费习惯、商业和配送。他们谈到了 Ask DoorDash 的推出，这是一个自然语言界面，既能推动餐厅发现，也能促进更大额的杂货订单。他们还讨论了 Dot，这是他们自主研发的自动驾驶配送机器人，已在凤凰城运营超过两年，并展示了他们在自动驾驶技术中面临并解决的运营和硬件挑战。Andy 和 Stanley 还谈到了自动驾驶配送中的“首尾 100 英尺问题”，为什么多模式策略是成功的关键，如何扩展自动驾驶和运营规模，以及为什么他们相信未来 DoorDash 需要更多而非更少的 Dasher。立即注册…
podcasts.originalExcerpt: DoorDash is not just a delivery company. From its inception, co-founders Andy Fang and Stanley Tang operated it as a robotics and autonomy company. Andy and Stanley join Sarah Guo to explain how autonomous tech and AI are reshaping consumer habits, commerce, and delivery. Andy and Stanley talk about the rollout of Ask DoorDash, a natural-language interface that’s driving both restaurant discovery and larger grocery orders. They also discuss Dot, their in-house autonomous delivery robot that has operated in Phoenix for over two years, and how it highlights the operational and hardware challenges they have faced and solved in autonomous tech. Andy and Stanley also speak about the “first and last 100 feet problem” in autonomous delivery, why multimodal strategies are the key to success, scaling autonomy and operations, and why they believe that more Dashers, not fewer, are the future of DoorDash.  Sign u…
