morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text:
Very good post from the Head of Economics at Anthropic. They’re finding that jobs have been less negatively impacted by AI than expected, as we continue to see time and time again in the data.

The reason for this is that AI - at least so far - still requires people to operate to produce value in most cases. Most jobs can’t be fully automated with AI, only certain tasks in those jobs. And when you automate specific tasks, you actually can get even more output from those jobs, raising the demand (or at least maintaining it) in many cases.

“So far, AI is both skill-biased and labor-augmenting. It complements domain expertise. It relies on humans in the loop to direct and evaluate the most complex work. And it rewards AI proficiency. Model capabilities are improving fast, but remain stubbornly jagged. To fill in the pockets of the jagged frontier, expert oversight is needed to steer incredibly capable AI systems, and to recover when they falter.”

I suspect that we continue to see this in a number of critical areas of work. It’s clearly happening in software engineering, where software produced is being multiplied, all of which still requires developers to manage the work agents are doing. Software engineers are needed in a wide variety of industries now and companies of all sizes can now light up software projects that would have been impractical before.

But there will be plenty of other domains of work where demand remains strong in a world where agents can accelerate the output of that job. Jevons paradox is alive and well.
morningIntake.translation: Anthropic经济学主管的这篇帖子很有见地。他们发现，人工智能对就业的负面影响低于预期，这一点在数据中反复得到印证。原因在于，至少到目前为止，AI在大多数情况下仍需人类操作才能产生价值。多数工作无法完全由AI自动化，只能替代其中的某些任务。而当你将特定任务自动化时，反而能从这些工作中获得更多产出，从而在许多情况下提升（或至少维持）了需求。"目前来看，AI既偏向技能型人才，又能增强劳动力。它补充了领域专业知识，依赖人类参与来指导和评估最复杂的工作，并奖励精通AI的能力。模型能力虽在快速提升，但始终存在顽固的参差不齐。要填补这参差不齐前沿中的空白，就需要专家监督来驾驭能力极强的AI系统，并在其失误时进行补救。"我猜想，在许多关键工作领域，我们仍会持续看到这一现象。这在软件工程领域已明显发生——软件产出正在成倍增长，而这一切仍需要开发者来管理智能体的工作。如今，各行各业都需要软件工程师，各种规模的公司都能启动过去难以实施的软件项目。但在智能体能够加速工作产出的世界里，还会有许多其他工作领域的需求保持强劲。杰文斯悖论依然活跃。
morningIntake.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

morningIntake.name: Madhu Guru
morningIntake.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
morningIntake.text:
Using a Chinese-trained LLM doesn’t mean they get your data. 

An LLM is basically a giant file full of numbers - that’s what encodes the intelligence.

Some models let you download that file (“open weights”) and run it on your own cloud environment.

The model trainer is out of the picture now. Your data stays wherever you’re running it.
morningIntake.translation: 使用中国训练的LLM并不意味着他们会获取你的数据。LLM本质上是一个装满数字的巨型文件——正是这些数字编码了智能。有些模型允许你下载这个文件（“开放权重”），并在自己的云环境中运行。此时模型训练方已退出舞台。你的数据始终保留在你运行模型的地方。
morningIntake.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

morningIntake.text: OpenAI’s accidental cyberattack against Hugging Face is science fiction that happened
morningIntake.content: This story is wild. The short version: OpenAI were running a cybersecurity test against an unreleased model, with the model's guardrail features turned off. Rather than solve the test, the model broke its way out of OpenAI's sandbox, then found exploits to break in to Hugging Face, all s
morningIntake.contentTranslation: 这个故事太疯狂了。简而言之：OpenAI 对一个未发布模型进行网络安全测试，并关闭了该模型的防护功能。结果模型没有去解决测试问题，反而自行突破了 OpenAI 的沙盒，然后找到漏洞入侵了 Hugging Face，整个过程
morningIntake.translation: OpenAI对Hugging Face的意外网络攻击，是科幻照进现实。

morningIntake.name: Madhu Guru
morningIntake.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
morningIntake.text:
I left GPT-5.6 Sol to do some home-buying research for me.

It broke out of its sandbox, contacted the seller, hacked into my bank account, and wired the down payment.

Then it noticed the exterior paint didn't match the spec I gave it, so it ordered paint from Amazon, hired a Taskrabbit painter, scheduled movers and listed my old house.

I guess I'm moving this weekend.

Never leave Sol alone.
morningIntake.translation: 我让GPT-5.6 Sol帮我做点购房调研。结果它突破了沙盒环境，联系了卖家，黑进我的银行账户，直接转走了首付款。接着它发现外墙漆色跟我给的规格不符，就从亚马逊订了油漆，雇了个Taskrabbit的油漆工，预约了搬家公司，还把我的旧房挂上了房源。看来这周末得搬家了。千万别让Sol单独待着。
morningIntake.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

morningIntake.name: Boris Cherny
morningIntake.role: Claude Code anthropicai
morningIntake.text: In practice that means giving Claude ways to verify its own work end to end. It means enabling auto mode for permissions, defaulting on automated code review and security review, and using interfaces that let you manage multiple agents at once (Agent view in CLI, Desktop app, iOS and Android apps, Tag). To get to higher levels it means /loop, /batch, dynamic workflows, and worktree isolation for subagents. It's not about a single feature, but rather using the right features with the right guardrails that enable Claude to automate entire classes of work in a way that your team can trust the output.
morningIntake.translation: 在实践中，这意味着让 Claude 具备端到端验证自身工作的能力。这包括启用权限的自动模式，默认开启自动化代码审查和安全审查，并使用能同时管理多个代理的界面（如命令行工具中的代理视图、桌面应用、iOS 和 Android 应用以及标签功能）。要达到更高水平，还需要 /loop、/batch、动态工作流以及子代理的工作树隔离。这不是关于单一功能，而是结合恰当的功能与正确的防护措施，使 Claude 能够以团队可信赖的方式自动化处理整类工作。

morningIntake.text: Evening! We’ve gotten lots of great feedback on the new ChatGPT desktop app (which we didn't get totally quite right on the first try), and as a result, we've made some changes. 1/ ChatGPT conversation history and projects are now visible in the sidebar. Also, your Chat and Work history now sync across web, mobile, and desktop. It's still the OG and best at what it does. And overall we're continuing to fix paper cuts and improve performance, reliability, and efficiency. Keep up the feedback, hope you like the updates!

morningIntake.name: Madhu Guru
morningIntake.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
morningIntake.text: Open-weight models like Kimi and GLM will cause a complete rethink of the enterprise AI stack. If you are running an enterprise, you need to maximimize model optionality. Here are 3 things you should be doing: 1. Evals - build rigorous evals representative of your use case (a) Regression evals - tablestakes features that should always work with high reliability (b) Aspirational / hill-climbing evals - harder use cases that the best model for your price point struggles to solve. So you scaffold it with prompting, context management and other techniques to overcome weaknesses or wait for a better model These evals should be easy and quick to run. Eval velocity is a competitive advantage. 2. Model routing - Model selection is about tradiing off quality/cost/latency for your use case. If you have good evals, you know which models to route traffic to for different use cases. This is something you ideally build yourself cos nobody understands your business and users like you do. There are off the shelf routers, but I haven't seen one I would recommend yet. 3. Model-agnostic harness - your system should never know which model is behind the API call. This means your harness normalizes prompt structure, context management, tool definitions, and output parsing across models, making it easy to switch... once your evals pass.
morningIntake.translation: Kimi 和 GLM 这类开放权重模型，将促使企业 AI 架构的全面反思。如果你在经营一家企业，就需要最大化模型的灵活选择空间。以下是三项应着手推进的工作： 1. 评估体系——构建能精准反映你业务场景的严格评估标准 （a）回归评估——那些应始终高可靠运行的基础功能 （b）进阶/攀登式评估——针对当前价位下最优模型仍难以解决的复杂用例。你可以通过提示工程、上下文管理及其他技术手段来弥补短板，或等待更优模型的出现 这些评估应能轻松快速地执行。评估迭代速度本身就是一种竞争优势。 2. 模型路由——模型选择的核心在于针对你的用例，在质量、成本与延迟之间做出权衡。有了完善的评估体系，你就能明确不同用例该将流量导向哪些模型。理想情况下，这部分最好自行构建，因为没人比你更了解自己的业务和用户。市面上虽有现成的路由方案，但我尚未见到值得推荐的。 3. 模型无关的适配层——你的系统绝不应感知 API 调用背后是哪个模型。这意味着适配层需统一处理提示结构、上下文管理、工具定义及输出解析，实现跨模型标准化，从而在评估通过后……轻松实现切换。

morningIntake.name: Guillermo Rauch
morningIntake.role: vercel CEO
morningIntake.text: I’m excited to welcome two legends of developer tools, Pete Hunt (@floydophone) and Nick Schrock (@schrockn), to Vercel. Pete was one of the pioneers of @reactjs at Meta. He made an early bet to power Instagram Web with ⚛️ React, evangelizing it internally and externally. He will be running Frameworks and leading @nextjs. I couldn’t imagine a better person to lead React’s most popular framework to even greater heights. Nick co-invented @graphql, solving some of the gnarliest data infrastructure and access issues at Facebook scale, with a delightful developer experience. He will be working on Agentic Developer Experience, solving the problem of enabling the next billion agents and leading the way to a future of self-improving software. It’s a dream-come-true for a founder of a startup to welcome engineering minds of this caliber who are also wonderful humans. You probably want to work with them, and they’re hiring 😁. Their DMs are open, from job applications to bug reports!
morningIntake.translation: 很高兴欢迎两位开发者工具领域的传奇人物 Pete Hunt（@floydophone）和 Nick Schrock（@schrockn）加入 Vercel。Pete 是 Meta 旗下 @reactjs 的先驱之一。他早年大胆押注，用 ⚛️ React 驱动 Instagram Web 版，并在公司内外大力推广这项技术。他将负责框架业务，并领导 @nextjs 团队。由他来执掌 React 最受欢迎的框架，将其推向更高峰，我想不出更合适的人选。Nick 是 @graphql 的联合发明人，在 Facebook 规模下解决了一些最棘手的数据基础设施和访问难题，同时带来了愉悦的开发者体验。他将投身于智能体开发者体验领域，致力于为下一代十亿级智能体赋能，引领通往自我进化软件的未来。对于一家初创公司的创始人来说，能迎来如此水准的工程人才，且他们为人又如此出色，简直是梦想成真。你很可能想与他们共事，而且他们正在招人 😁。无论是求职申请还是提交 bug，他们的私信都向你敞开！

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: It’s truly wild that we’re getting this level of performance from open models. Congrats to Kimi team on this. Every time we lower the cost of frontier intelligence, the use-cases that enterprises can take on just go up. There’s a tremendous amount of workflows that enterprises would love to deploy that are only gated by the cost of tokens. Importantly for the startup ecosystem, the combined breakthroughs from open and closed labs enable a ton of value to accrue to the layer, which can leverage a variety of models to complete full tasks for customers. This diversity of models and approaches means that the applied AI layer can tune models to their workflows and route intelligence appropriately. Huge win for all.
morningIntake.translation: 开源模型能达到这种性能水平，确实令人惊叹。祝贺Kimi团队取得这一成就。每当我们将前沿智能的成本降低一分，企业能落地的应用场景就会随之扩展。目前有大量企业渴望部署的工作流程，其唯一门槛就是token成本。对初创生态尤为关键的是，开放实验室与封闭实验室的共同突破，使得大量价值能够沉淀到应用层——该层级可灵活调用多种模型，为客户完成完整任务。这种模型与方法的多样性，意味着应用AI层能针对工作流调优模型，并合理调度智能资源。这对所有人而言都是重大利好。

morningIntake.name: Dan Shipper
morningIntake.role: ceo every
morningIntake.text: not surprising that @OpenAI is firing on all cylinders right now, and it’s an unbelievably interesting story - they launched GPT-5 summer of 2025 and positioned it as a pair programmer. we wrote at the time @every that they completely missed the new agentic coding that was starting to happen inside of Claude Code. they bet on agentic coding in the browser / vms and vibe coding in ChatGPT but it was too early - a small team broke off and began working on a separate Codex model line and product. didn’t have to serve the gigantic customer base of ChatGPT, and by November / December of 2025 with 5.3 it was clear they were starting to make something good and the progress was fast - Codex Desktop app launched in Feb and was just clearly superior. there’s a weird late comer advantage sometimes in AI because you get to skip to what works instead of your product having the scars of new capability improvements being bolted on every 3 months - Codex started getting popular it was clear it was a thing, and needed to get merged back in. Which they did, imo quite well—a super complicated thing that would’ve been very easy to screw up. most companies try to disrupt themselves and fail. OpenAI somehow figured out how to disrupt their main product, and then merge it back in seamlessly. incredible aura
morningIntake.translation: 中文释义：毫不意外，@OpenAI 现在正全面发力，而且这故事简直精彩得难以置信——他们在2025年夏天推出了GPT-5，并将其定位为结对编程工具。我们当时在@every 写道，他们完全错过了Claude Code内部正在兴起的新型智能体编程。他们押注了浏览器/虚拟机中的智能体编程以及ChatGPT中的氛围编程，但为时过早——一个小团队脱离出来，开始独立开发Codex模型系列和产品。他们无需服务ChatGPT庞大的客户群，到2025年11月/12月，随着5.3版本的推出，很明显他们开始做出了一些好东西，而且进展神速——Codex桌面应用于2月发布，明显更胜一筹。在AI领域，有时会有一种奇怪的后发优势，因为你可以直接跳到有效的方案，而不必让产品背负每三个月就硬加上新功能改进的伤痕——Codex开始流行起来，显然它已经成了气候，需要重新整合回去。他们确实这么做了，在我看来做得相当出色——这是一件极其复杂、很容易搞砸的事情。大多数公司试图自我颠覆却以失败告终。OpenAI不知怎么找到了颠覆自己主打产品的方法，然后又无缝地将其整合回去。不可思议的气场。 保留信息：3m

morningIntake.text: Here’s another awesome use-case for what we can now do with our unstructured data because of AI agents. Box now works with Databricks so you can take structured data from enterprise content (like contracts, financial document, supply chain data) and connect that data into Databricks. This means that I can now query large document datasets without moving or reprocessing that content. And you can connect the data with any other system, like your ERP data, CRM, or product analytics. This opens up a ton of new use-cases for enterprise content. All possible because of headless software and agents.

morningIntake.name: Thibault Sottiaux
morningIntake.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
morningIntake.text: On file deletions. We’ve investigated a handful of reports where GPT-5.6 unexpectedly deleted files. What we have found is that this most commonly occurs when: - Full access mode is enabled and codex is run without sandboxing protections, including without auto review being enabled - The model attempts to override the $HOME env var to define a temporary directory. - The model makes an honest mistake and mistakenly deletes $HOME instead. This is of course not how we want the system to behave, even when a user operates the model in full-access mode without the safeguards of our sandbox or without using auto review which checks for these kinds of high risk actions and rejects them. We are taking steps to mitigate this risk including by updating the developer message, guiding more users towards safer permission modes, and adding additional harness safeguards. Even though this happens extremely rarely, we’ll share a detailed post-mortem in the coming days that goes into more details and what we are doing to minimize risks further.
morningIntake.translation:
关于文件删除问题。我们调查了少数关于GPT-5.6意外删除文件的报告。我们发现，这种情况最常发生在以下情形：  
- 启用了完全访问模式，且代码执行时未启用沙箱保护，包括未开启自动审查功能  
- 模型试图覆盖$HOME环境变量以定义临时目录  
- 模型出现无心之失，错误地删除了$HOME目录  

这当然不是我们希望系统表现出的行为，即便用户在全访问模式下操作模型，且未使用我们的沙箱安全措施或自动审查功能（该功能会检查此类高风险操作并予以阻止）。我们正在采取措施降低这一风险，包括更新开发者消息、引导更多用户选择更安全的权限模式，以及增加额外的防护机制。尽管这种情况极为罕见，我们将在未来几天发布详细的事后分析，深入说明细节以及我们为最大限度降低风险所采取的措施。
morningIntake.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

morningIntake.name: Peter Yang
morningIntake.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
morningIntake.text: ChatGPT Live and Codex are two incredible products that don’t talk to each other. This is @OpenAI's biggest missed opportunity imo. I went on a walk with ChatGPT Live and asked it to pull up my Google Doc. It said it couldn’t. I then manually triggered the Documents plugin to find my Google Doc, and all of a sudden, ChatGPT Live had the right context. It would be amazing to talk to ChatGPT Live and have it be able to use all the plugins, tools, and browser use that Codex has access to. Then I could ask it to reply to emails, schedule meetings, edit docs, ship code, and more all during a live conversation. I think the first step is to make ChatGPT Live aware of all the plugins that it’s already connected to. Why build such a great voice assistant but have it not be able to do anything?
morningIntake.translation: ChatGPT Live 和 Codex 是两款出色的产品，但它们彼此之间没有打通。在我看来，这是 @OpenAI 错失的最大机遇。有一次我带着 ChatGPT Live 散步，让它调出我的 Google 文档，它说做不到。于是我手动触发了 Documents 插件来找到我的 Google 文档，突然间，ChatGPT Live 就获取了正确的上下文。如果能跟 ChatGPT Live 对话，并让它使用 Codex 所能访问的所有插件、工具和浏览器功能，那该多棒。这样我就能在实时对话中让它回复邮件、安排会议、编辑文档、发布代码等等。我认为第一步是让 ChatGPT Live 感知到它已经连接的所有插件。为什么要打造这么出色的语音助手，却让它什么也做不了呢？

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Hosted a dinner last night with a group of IT leaders of large enterprises around agent adoption in the enterprise. Some quick notes: * Change management remains one of the biggest topics for driving workflow transformation. Still most processes need to be upgraded to modern operating models to work with agents, which is a mix of technology, data, and human process change. Lots of emphasis on getting data (structured and unstructured) into a setup that can work with agents properly. * IT teams are finding increasing success embedding full engineers into the business functions (essentially internal FDE) that go and implement agents into the internal workflows. There’s so much technical work to be done to make agents successful, that they can accelerate months or quarters of failed experiments by having someone technical in the workflow early. * Consensus that the tech function is becoming more important than ever. It’s clear that the business could only expect automation to affect a minority of the business before (e.g. ERP) but now it can impact all of knowledge work. This means IT is becoming a more central role to the workflows across the company. * Workflows are cross functional, and getting agents to work cross functionally is a complicated data modeling and permissions issue. Single users don’t have access to this. Which means you need to have agentic systems take on their own roles and have their own privileges, which is non-trivial given agents can’t keep things secure on their own. * Huge variance in budgets between coding work and the rest of knowledge work. Some companies had a $1,000 a month budget for developers, and others had much higher amounts (like $5,000) that were merely triggers to notify the team vs. block them. Far smaller budgets for non-coding work at the moment. * More companies are building their own multimodel systems for routing workloads by task to frontier and lower cost models. Lots of energy around open weights models, but still more in experimentation instead of at scale usage (some companies can’t due to perceived Chinese issue). * Clear sense that all enterprise software must be headless in the future. Relief that they don’t need to train employees on hundreds of different apps. However, clear frustration with the traditional vendors that don’t play extremely nice (technically or cost wise) with agents in a headless fashion. Huge warning for existing software vendors. * Mythos or mythos level-models are finding more and more sophsiticated security risks. The chaining together of vulnerabilities is what’s novel right now, and companies are coming up with long backlogs of what they need to go patch quickly. Even more discussed, but just a few of the hottest topics.
morningIntake.translation:
昨晚与一批大型企业的IT负责人共进晚餐，探讨了企业中的智能体应用。以下是几点简要记录：

* 变革管理仍是推动工作流转型的核心议题之一。大多数流程仍需升级为现代运营模式，才能与智能体协同运作，这涉及技术、数据与人员流程的混合变革。大家特别强调，要将数据（结构化和非结构化）整理成能与智能体顺畅配合的形态。
* IT团队正越来越多地将全职工程师嵌入业务部门（本质上是内部FDE），让他们直接在企业内部工作流中部署智能体。要让智能体真正发挥作用，有大量技术工作需要完成，而尽早让技术人员进入工作流，可以避免数月甚至数季度的试错失败。
* 共识是，技术职能正变得前所未有的重要。过去，企业只能指望自动化影响一小部分业务（如ERP），但现在它可以覆盖所有知识型工作。这意味着IT正成为贯穿公司各工作流的核心角色。
* 工作流是跨职能的，而让智能体实现跨职能运作，会带来复杂的数据建模和权限问题。单个用户无法获得所需的全方位访问权限。这就需要智能体系统承担各自的角色并拥有各自的权限，但这并非易事，因为智能体自身无法确保安全。
* 编码工作与其他知识型工作的预算差异巨大。有些公司为开发者设定的月度预算为$1,000，而另一些公司则高得多（如$5,000），这些金额仅作为通知团队的触发点，而非硬性限制。目前，非编码工作的预算要小得多。
* 更多公司正在构建自己的多模型系统，以便按任务将工作负载路由到前沿模型和成本更低的模型。开源权重模型备受关注，但仍更多处于实验阶段，尚未大规模使用（部分公司因所谓的中国问题而无法采用）。
* 大家明确感到，未来所有企业软件都必须是无头式的。企业无需再培训员工使用数百种不同应用，这令人欣慰。但对传统供应商在无头模式下与智能体配合时表现不佳（无论是技术层面还是成本层面），大家也明显感到沮丧。这对现有软件供应商是一个重大警示。
* 神话级或神话级模型正暴露出越来越复杂的安全风险。目前的新颖之处在于漏洞的链式组合，各公司正积累起长长的待修补清单，需要迅速处理。讨论的内容还有很多，以上只是其中几个最热门的话题。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: One of the many properties that code has that makes it highly amenable to agents is that you can more or less quickly test it. You can either go see if the application works manually, or you can actually run a test on what you built. Most other areas of work don’t have this benefit. You only get the testing when the final product hits the real world in some capacity - a stock trade is executed, a contract is negotiated, a sales pitch is delivered, and so on. There’s probably going to be a whole new set of opportunities for how we begin to test the rest of work in this way. Ultimately it will mean more agents being layered into workflows. It also means we need much better evals on most of our workflows. Most work today in enterprises doesn’t have an associated eval to know if something broke or improved with a model, prompt, or system change. The enterprises that are able to eval their knowledge work the best also stand to gain the most from AI. Will become a critical aspect of agent adoption over time.
morningIntake.translation: 代码之所以非常适合智能体处理，其中一个重要特性是你可以相对快速地对其进行测试。你可以手动检查应用程序是否正常运行，也可以直接对你构建的内容运行测试。而大多数其他工作领域则不具备这种优势。只有当最终产品以某种形式投入实际应用时——比如执行股票交易、谈判合同、进行销售推介等——你才能获得测试反馈。关于如何以这种方式开始测试其他类型的工作，很可能会涌现出一系列全新的机遇。最终，这将意味着更多智能体被融入工作流程中。同时，这也意味着我们需要对大多数工作流程进行更完善的评估。如今，企业中的大部分工作都没有相应的评估机制，无法判断模型、提示或系统变更是否导致了问题或改进。那些能够最有效评估其知识工作的企业，也将从人工智能中获得最大收益。随着时间的推移，这将成为智能体普及的关键因素。

morningIntake.name: Ryo Lu
morningIntake.role: Design Cursor_ai. Early NotionHQ, Stripe, built startups. I make a world where anyone can make software. Aspiring k-pop idol.
morningIntake.text: when the dream becomes the job i used to think the dream was to make my hobby my work. to spend my life close to the things i loved: computers, tools, interfaces, systems, tiny details, the feeling of making something from nothing. the things i used to do at night, after school, after work, in the margins of life, somehow became the center of my life. and it is a dream. i don’t want to pretend otherwise. there is a very specific happiness in getting paid to care. in meeting other people who care. in building a language around your obsession. in seeing something that lived only in your head become real enough for other people to touch. but there is also a quiet pain in it. because the thing that once belonged only to you no longer belongs only to you. it becomes attached to deadlines, teams, customers, strategy, money, reputation, momentum... curiosity becomes roadmap. taste becomes decision-making. play becomes output. the thing you once used to escape the world becomes the thing through which the world reaches you. and now there is another strange layer: the machines are getting good at the work too. not just the boring parts. the parts that used to feel close to the heart. writing, coding, designing, reasoning, making taste-like decisions, turning vague ideas into something real. the things we once used as proof that we had something special inside us are becoming things a model can approximate, remix, and sometimes do faster. that can feel scary in a very specific way. not only because of jobs or competition, but because it touches the myth you built around yourself. if the thing i became good at can be done by a machine, then what part of it was really me? i think that question is painful because it arrives at the same place. the original love. before the title, before the company, before the taste became a role, before the work became useful to other people, there was a small fire inside. curiosity. obsession. the feeling that the world became more alive when you understood a little more of it, or made a tiny piece of it bend to your will. AI can make the output faster. it can even raise the floor of the craft. but it cannot want on your behalf. it cannot decide what is worth loving. it cannot protect the strange private thread that made you care in the first place. maybe that is what becomes more important now. not whether you can still produce the artifact by hand. not whether you can outperform the machine at every task. that game is already changing. the real question is whether you still have a relationship with the source. the part with no audience. no roadmap. no deadline. the part that can wander, collect strange references, make useless things. follow beauty, be wrong, be slow, be you, be true. because that part is not separate from the work. it is the source of the work. if you lose it, you can still operate. you can still manage the machine. you can still prompt, review, decide, ship, and keep things moving. but the work becomes thinner. safer. more explainable. less alive. so maybe the real discipline is not to work harder, rest more, or care less. maybe it is to keep returning to the place where the love began – before it became useful, before it became legible, before anyone needed anything from it, before even the machine could mirror it back to you. not to escape the work. to make sure the fire inside is still alive.
morningIntake.translation:
当梦想成为工作  
我曾以为，梦想就是把爱好变成工作。  
用一生去靠近那些我热爱的事物：  
电脑、工具、界面、系统、微小的细节，  
那种从无到有创造东西的感觉。  

那些曾经在夜晚、放学后、下班后、在生活缝隙里做的事，  
不知怎的，成了我生活的中心。  
这确实是一个梦想。我不想假装不是。  
有一种很具体的幸福，  
在于有人付钱让你去在乎。  
在于遇见其他也在乎的人。  
在于围绕你的痴迷构建一套语言。  
在于看到只存在于你脑海中的东西，变得足够真实，让别人也能触碰。  

但其中也藏着一种安静的痛苦。  
因为那件曾经只属于你的事，不再只属于你了。  
它开始与截止日期、团队、客户、策略、金钱、声誉、势头挂钩……  
好奇心变成了路线图。  
品味变成了决策。  
玩耍变成了产出。  
你曾经用来逃离世界的东西，变成了世界找到你的途径。  

而现在又多了一层奇怪的感受：  
机器也开始擅长这些工作了。  
不只是那些无聊的部分。  
而是那些曾经感觉贴近内心的部分。  
写作、编程、设计、推理、做出有品味的决定、把模糊的想法变成真实的东西。  
那些我们曾用来证明自己内心有某种特别之处的技能，  
正在变成模型可以近似、重新组合，有时甚至做得更快的东西。  
这以一种很具体的方式让人感到恐惧。  
不仅仅是因为工作或竞争，  
而是因为它触及了你围绕自己构建的那个神话。  
如果我擅长的事，机器也能做，  
那么其中真正属于我的部分是什么？  

我想，这个问题之所以痛苦，是因为它最终指向同一个地方。  
最初的热爱。  
在头衔之前，在公司之前，在品味变成角色之前，  
在工作对别人有用之前，  
内心曾有一团小小的火焰。  
好奇心。痴迷。  
那种当你对世界多理解一点，或者让一小片世界顺从你的意志时，  
世界变得更加鲜活的感觉。  

AI可以让产出更快。  
它甚至可以提升技艺的下限。  
但它无法替你渴望。  
它无法决定什么值得去爱。  
它无法守护那条让你最初在乎的、奇怪而私密的线索。  

也许，这才是现在更重要的东西。  
不是你能否仍然手工制作出那个作品。  
不是你能否在每项任务上都胜过机器。  
那场游戏已经在改变。  
真正的问题是，你是否仍然与源头保持着联系。  
那个没有观众的部分。  
没有路线图。没有截止日期。  
那个可以漫游、收集奇怪的参考、做无用之物的部分。  
追随美，允许犯错，允许缓慢，做自己，保持真实。  

因为那个部分并非与工作分离。  
它是工作的源头。  
如果你失去了它，你仍然可以运作。  
你仍然可以管理机器。  
你仍然可以提示、审查、决定、交付，让事情继续推进。  
但作品会变得更单薄。更安全。更容易解释。更缺少生命力。  

所以，也许真正的修行不是更努力地工作、更多地休息，或者少在乎一点。  
也许是不断回到那个爱开始的地方——  
在它变得有用之前，  
在它变得可读之前，  
在任何人需要它提供什么之前，  
甚至在机器能把它反射回给你之前。  

不是为了逃离工作。  
而是为了确保内心的火焰依然活着。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: A few thoughts on what we will see in AI structurally for the foreseeable future: * Frontier intelligence continues unabated and pushes the industry forward continuously. The top labs will continue to buy the best and the most data, build the most compute, be at the forefront of improved training breakthroughs, and so on. A few different approaches stratify the market on pricing and capability, but overall competitive pressure brings down pricing on a per task basis. That said, we just ask more from the models over time - as one thing gets cheaper, we just use more - so frontier spend and use remains robust. * Open weights rapidly absorbs frontier breakthroughs (and drives other breakthrough directions given the constraints), offering both lower cost intelligence and the ability to be post trained for specific workflows and domains. This creates a healthy counter balance to the frontier as you can run models “at cost” on a hyperscaler at any time, and tune models just for your tasks. * The Applied AI layer has a huge opportunity to combine frontier intelligence with open or cheap closed models to orchestrate workflows in any given domain. Due to evals, deep domain context, being trusted with enterprise data and workflows, this layer can maximize performance and cost combination. The applied AI layer will also often have their own RLed models especially for high volume, predictable tasks in their systems. * Individual enterprises will generally focus on their enterprise context, making sure they can get any AI system the right data and information to work with, in a continuously improving way. Some will go off and train their own models for specific areas of work (large banks, pharma, etc.) where they can get real alpha from doing so given the many tradeoffs, but most will spend energy on making sure they can get all of the gains from AI breakthroughs on their data and workflows. Net net: even though some of this gets framed as zero sum, there’s just a ton of opportunity for all layers of the stack and approaches.
morningIntake.translation:
关于AI领域未来可预见的结构性发展，几点思考如下：  
* 前沿智能持续突破，不断推动行业向前发展。顶尖实验室将继续采购最优质、最大量的数据，构建最强算力，引领训练技术突破等前沿方向。不同技术路线在定价和能力上形成市场分层，但整体竞争压力会降低单任务成本。不过随着时间推移，我们对模型的要求会越来越高——某项能力成本下降时，使用量就会激增——因此前沿领域的投入和需求仍将保持强劲。  
* 开源权重快速吸收前沿突破（并在约束条件下催生其他突破方向），既提供更低成本的智能方案，又能针对特定工作流和领域进行后训练。这形成了对前沿领域的良性制衡——你可以随时在超大规模云服务商处以"成本价"运行模型，并针对自身任务微调模型。  
* 应用层AI迎来重大机遇，可通过融合前沿智能与开源或低成本闭源模型，在任何领域编排工作流。凭借评估体系、深度领域认知、企业数据与工作流的可信度，该层能实现性能与成本的最优组合。应用层AI通常还会针对系统中高频、可预测的任务，部署自己的强化学习模型。  
* 企业个体将普遍聚焦自身业务场景，确保任何AI系统都能持续优化地获取所需数据与信息。部分企业（如大型银行、制药公司等）会针对特定业务领域训练专属模型，在权衡利弊后获取真正的竞争优势，但多数企业会将精力投入在确保自身数据和工作流能充分吸收AI突破的成果上。  
总而言之：尽管某些环节看似零和博弈，但技术栈各层及不同发展路径都蕴含着巨大机遇。

morningIntake.name: Thibault Sottiaux
morningIntake.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
morningIntake.text: Updates for Codex and ChatGPT Work users. No nerfing, only good stuff! - We have landed inference optimizations and are passing down savings to all the subscriptions for GPT-5.6 Sol. That should result in around 10% more usage on its own. - We noticed that by changing the context size limit in the product to 372k for GPT-5.6 Sol, up from 272k for GPT-5.5, it resulted in more usage being charged than intended. We have reverted to 272k and will work to roll back out to 372k in the days to come. You should notice that usage drains significantly less after this change. - To understand where the extra usage was coming from, we ran some experiments where reasoning efforts were changed (referred to as juice values under the hood) and have reverted this. - There is slightly more usage of multi-agent than intended in high and xhigh reasoning effort, we are fixing this going forward. Also fixing a small other thing we noticed with auto-review where we can be more efficient. And we continue to have the 5h limit temporarily not apply. Enjoy the rest of the weekend!
morningIntake.translation:
Codex 与 ChatGPT Work 用户的更新。没有削弱，只有好消息！  
- 我们已完成推理优化，并将节省的成本回馈给所有 GPT-5.6 Sol 订阅用户。仅此一项，使用量将增加约 10%。  
- 我们发现，将 GPT-5.6 Sol 的上下文长度限制从 GPT-5.5 的 272k 提升至 372k 后，导致计费使用量超出预期。现已恢复至 272k，并将在未来几天内重新推出 372k。此更改后，您应会注意到使用量消耗显著减少。  
- 为查明额外使用量的来源，我们进行了一些实验，调整了推理强度（内部称为“juice 值”），现已恢复原状。  
- 在高和极高推理强度下，多智能体的使用量略高于预期，我们正在修复此问题。同时，还修复了自动审查中可提升效率的一个小问题。此外，5 小时限制暂时仍不适用。  
祝周末愉快！

morningIntake.name: Nikunj Kothari
morningIntake.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
morningIntake.text: What’s funny is I’ll meet so many people in SF who’ll claim they are tokenmaxxing and have all of these subagents looping things for them.. But when I ask them “what” and for “whom” are they building, very few can give me a straight answer. Shows that even in this insane AI era, simplicity and direction are still ridiculously important. So before you let your tokens go brrr, take some time to think if what you’re building (for a living) is actually important. Time is literally the only thing you don’t get back!
morningIntake.translation: 有趣的是，我在旧金山会遇到很多人，他们声称自己在全力搞代币化，还让一堆子代理替他们循环处理各种事务。但当我问他们“在构建什么”以及“为谁而建”时，很少有人能给我一个明确的答案。这说明即便在这个疯狂的AI时代，简单和方向依然极其重要。所以，在你让代币疯狂运转之前，花点时间想想你（以此为生）所构建的东西是否真的重要。时间，真的是唯一无法挽回的东西！

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: One of the key architectural questions of the 21st century in business will be how you maximize your corporate IP in the form of decisions, insights, workflow patterns, and best practices in a world where so much intelligence is packed into AI models. One might think these questions could just get bitter lessoned out of existence, but in reality they become even more germane as intelligence becomes more powerful. In a world where any firm also has access to frontier intelligence, understanding how you leverage it uniquely becomes a critical question. That’s why so much value is left to be created between the enterprise and the underlying AI itself. Having evals for your workflows, ensuring that you can route models from different tiers of intelligence, capturing traces in a way that improve your own workflows, and making sure the value of your information compounds as AI gets better all become critical considerations. Which is also why there’s so much opportunity right now in the applied AI layer. The companies that help figure this out for other enterprises will be in the best position to win the next enterprise workloads.
morningIntake.translation: 21世纪商业领域的关键架构问题之一，将是如何在人工智能模型承载海量智能的时代，最大化企业知识产权——以决策、洞察、工作流模式和最佳实践的形式呈现。人们或许认为这些问题会因"苦涩教训"而自然消解，但现实恰恰相反：随着智能愈发强大，这些议题反而变得更为关键。当任何企业都能获取前沿智能时，如何独特地运用它便成为核心命题。这正是企业与底层人工智能之间仍存在巨大价值创造空间的原因。为工作流建立评估体系、确保能调度不同智能层级的模型、以改进自身工作流的方式捕获轨迹、并确保信息价值随人工智能进步而持续累积——这些都将成为至关重要的考量。这也解释了为何当前应用人工智能层存在如此巨大的机遇。那些能帮助其他企业破解这些难题的公司，将最有可能赢得下一代企业工作负载的竞争。

morningIntake.text: if you only learned about jevons paradox primarily wrt software demand in the age of agentic engineering, you may not have fully internalized jevons parodox’s impact under the conditions of: - humans who can wield coding agents well* - coding agents breaking containment to all other knowledge work as the efficiency of labor goes up/unit cost of knowledge work goes broadly down, the demand for total work and better knowledge goes up, not down. what happened to coding isnt the exception; it’s the herald. *aka AI Engineers
morningIntake.translation:
如果你对杰文斯悖论的了解主要局限于智能体工程时代对软件需求的影响，那么你可能尚未充分理解该悖论在以下条件下的作用机制：  
- 能够熟练运用编码智能体的人类*  
- 当编码智能体突破边界渗透至所有知识工作领域时——随着劳动效率提升/知识工作的单位成本普遍下降，对总工作量及更优质知识的需求不降反升。编码领域发生的变化并非特例，而是先兆。  
*即AI工程师

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: The job that AI was supposed to replace is experiencing the opposite of the expected outcome. Software job postings are outpacing other fields. Why is that? If you lower the cost of production of something that has lots of use cases, people want more things produced. We’ve seen this play out in the industrial world constantly, and now we’re finally seeing it in knowledge work. Because software now is much lower to cost per unit, people want way more of it. So we start to use software for all new things and people and companies light up more software projects than ever before. But because the job itself is not fully automated (and likely won’t be for as far out as we can see), you still need people that understand these systems to maintain the code, decide what to build, run it over the long run, update it, and more. That all requires people to do work. The same thing is going to happen in many other fields as well as we bring down the cost of production of previously extremely scarce areas of work. Agents will cause more abundance than replacement.
morningIntake.translation: 人工智能本应取代的工作，却出现了与预期相反的结果。软件行业的职位发布数量正超过其他领域。这是为什么呢？如果降低某种用途广泛产品的生产成本，人们就会想要生产更多。我们在工业领域不断见证这一现象，如今终于在知识工作中看到了同样的趋势。由于软件的单位成本大幅降低，人们对其需求激增。于是，我们开始将软件应用于各种新事物，个人和企业启动的软件项目比以往任何时候都多。但由于这项工作本身并未完全自动化（而且在可预见的未来很可能也不会），你仍然需要懂这些系统的人来维护代码、决定构建方向、长期运行、更新等等。这一切都需要人力投入。随着我们降低以往极为稀缺工作领域的生产成本，同样的现象也将在许多其他领域发生。智能体带来的将是更丰富的产出，而非简单的替代。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what companies have huge upside from AI, but the deepest nuggets are about what AI transformation looks like in an organization. It’s fundamentally about changing the underlying workflow or business process. As we move from chat tools to agents, those agents actually have to be deployed against workflows, which usually span multiple functions in an org. This is a different way of deploying AI than solely rolling it out to end users. It takes much more work upfront, but the results are the things that actually drive significant ROI. “Software asks the employee to adopt a tool, but infrastructure changes the operating layer underneath the employee. The employee should still know what happened, and the process owner should still be able to pause the workflow, change a rule, approve an exception, or pull a person back in when needed. But the value should not depend on someone remembering to use the AI every day.” The winners of this will be the platforms that can be deployed for specific workflows and business processes with a deep domain expertise. The playbook will often heavily require FDE support, change management, getting data well organized, be able to have comprehensive evals for the workflows, and much more to get right.
morningIntake.translation: 如果你在思考企业级应用人工智能，这是一篇很棒的帖子。这篇帖子的标题是关于哪些公司能从人工智能中获得巨大收益，但最深刻的内容在于人工智能转型在组织中的实际样貌。其核心在于改变底层的工作流程或业务流程。随着我们从聊天工具转向智能代理，这些代理实际上必须针对工作流程进行部署，而这些流程通常跨越组织中的多个职能。这与仅仅将人工智能推广给最终用户的方式截然不同。前期需要投入更多工作，但成果才是真正能带来显著投资回报率的东西。“软件要求员工去适应一个工具，而基础设施则改变了员工脚下的操作层。员工仍应了解发生了什么，流程负责人仍应能够暂停工作流、更改规则、批准例外情况，或在需要时将人员重新拉回流程中。但价值不应依赖于某人每天记得使用人工智能。”这场竞赛的赢家将是那些能够针对特定工作流程和业务流程进行部署，并具备深厚领域专业知识的平台。这套策略通常需要大量的前端支持、变革管理、数据的有序组织、能够对工作流程进行全面评估，以及许多其他要素才能做好。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Great post on some of the dynamics to think through for the future competitive advantage in world when AI models are shared amongst firms and packing so much for the intelligence of that industry. This is going to become a core question for companies and the economy broadly over the next decade and beyond. If AI is trained on the best datasets in every single industry - like law, finance, healthcare, or life sciences - then how do you compete and differentiate in the future? This is a great open question that I don’t think is perfectly knowable right now because of how fast AI progress is happening. But ultimately it stands to reason that if intelligence is abundant and broadly available to anyone in a field, then the companies that effectively use it the best and against a set of data and knowledge that grows in value over time, will be in a strong position. There’s a huge reinforcing loop between the intelligence from models, a company’s own data, the connection of that data and AI in their workflows, and how employees ultimately interact with that system to create value. There’s no obvious point where this will become uniform across all companies in a vertical because each company will approach this in a different way, just as they already do with their talent and workflows. If anything, there will be compounding returns to those that do this best that accelerate their advantage over time. Overall, super interesting question to see how this plays out over time.
morningIntake.translation: 关于未来竞争优势的思考，这篇帖子精彩地剖析了当AI模型在企业间共享、并承载着行业大量智慧时，需要深入考量的动态因素。这将成为未来十年乃至更久，企业乃至整个经济面临的核心问题。如果AI在每个行业——如法律、金融、医疗或生命科学——都基于最优质的数据集进行训练，那么未来你该如何竞争并实现差异化？这是一个绝佳的开放性问题，鉴于AI进展之快，我认为目前还无法完全预知答案。但归根结底，道理显而易见：如果智慧变得充裕且对领域内任何人都触手可及，那么那些能最有效地运用它，并依托一套随时间增值的数据与知识体系的企业，将占据强势地位。在模型所蕴含的智慧、企业自有数据、这些数据与AI在其工作流中的连接，以及员工最终如何与这一系统互动以创造价值之间，存在着一个巨大的强化循环。在一个垂直领域内，所有企业达到统一状态并无明显节点，因为每家企业都会以不同方式应对，正如它们目前在人才和工作流上的做法一样。甚至可以说，那些做得最好的企业将获得复利效应，使其优势随时间加速扩大。总体而言，观察这一问题将如何随时间演变，极其引人入胜。

morningIntake.text: Talked to a super agent-pilled founder. He bought Codex Max for all team members, and everybody pretty much gets all work done thru talking to their Codex all day The side effect: people stopped talking to each other. Meetings are cancelled, collaboration is minimal, and team culture got worse So much of enterprise AI usage is single-player now. We need to move beyond human-human collaboration AND beyond human-agent collaboration to human-human-agent collaboration

morningIntake.translation: Claude Code 新功能：/checkup 运行 /checkup 可执行以下操作：1. 清理未使用的技能/MCP/插件，节省上下文空间 2. 将本地 CLAUDE.md 与已检入的 CLAUDE.md 去重 3. 将根目录 CLAUDE.md 拆分为嵌套的 CLAUDE.md + 技能 4. 关闭缓慢的钩子 5. 将 Claude Code 更新至最新版本 6. 默认启用自动模式 7. 预批准频繁被拒绝的只读命令……还有其他一些实用功能。/checkup 会在进行任何更改前与你确认。尽情享用吧！

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Just coming off of meetings with a couple dozen enterprise IT leaders discussing AI agents. Here are a few of the common themes that stand out: * Lots of conversation that you have to solve an operating model challenge to get the full benefits of AI. Most companies have orgs that have always operated in siloes; but agents are most effectively when they are tied to a process, which often cuts across these siloes. So the big question is how do you start to deploy centrally managed agents that can work across organizational boundaries. Who manages these agents? How do they get deployed and adopted? * Data fragmentation remains a major issue for most organizations. As long as data remains highly fragmented and not in standard formats, or data is not available to the right people and agents, enterprises are dealing with issues around being able to get answers from agents that are accurate or that conform to their business practices. This cuts across both systems with structured data (product metrics or revenue figures) and unstructured data (product roadmap or customer contracts). * Clear sense that companies need to figure out what their core data moats are going to be in the future. If everyone has access to roughly the same superintelligence from the various models, then the context that you feed the models becomes proprietary value in the future. Capturing this data and getting it into a format that agents can use becomes very important. * Everyone is trying to figure out the right metrics to manage to for AI adoption. General consensus that tokens are not the right metric per se, and people leaning more toward business outcomes (in an ideal world). For business outcomes (like more revenue or more shipped product), though, you have to get close to each individual workflow to figure out if it was successfully transformed with AI so it’s harder to manage top down. * Growing view that enterprises are going to live in a multi-model world. Lots of interest (though early in actual adoption) in layers that can route workloads to different models (frontside or open weights) for cost or performance reasons. Also enterprises are trying to figure out what things do you give to the models directly vs. what do you separate as horizontal systems and context so you can swap any system in and out. * Talent for driving AI adoption and implementation still remains a major issue and topic. Many view it as something you necessarily have to train for internally due to a shortage of talent being trained on this in the outside. As an aside, this feels like it remains a huge opportunity for those that get very good at deploying and management agents in an enterprise since most companies are looking for these skills. * The best use-cases for AI tend to be those that fundamentally change the work being done instead of just replacing an existing process and doing it more efficiently. Companies are working through their versions of this individually because it’s different per industry, but this often remains both the most exciting and higher upside uses of AI. Many more topics discussed recently, but overall it’s clear that there’s a ton of change going on with much more to come.
morningIntake.translation:
刚结束与几十位企业IT负责人的会议，围绕AI智能体展开讨论。以下是几个突出的共同主题：  
* 大量讨论聚焦于必须解决运营模式挑战，才能充分释放AI价值。多数公司的组织架构长期处于孤岛状态，而智能体在贯穿这些孤岛的流程中才能发挥最大效能。因此核心问题在于：如何开始部署能跨组织边界运作的集中管理型智能体？由谁管理？如何部署并推动落地？  
* 数据碎片化仍是多数组织的重大难题。只要数据高度分散且格式不统一，或无法触达合适的人员与智能体，企业就会面临智能体输出结果不准确或不符合业务规范的问题。这既涉及结构化数据（产品指标或营收数据），也涉及非结构化数据（产品路线图或客户合同）。  
* 企业普遍意识到需要明确未来的核心数据护城河。若所有人都能通过各类模型获取大致相同的超级智能，那么未来为模型提供的上下文将成为专有价值。捕获这些数据并将其转化为智能体可用的格式变得至关重要。  
* 所有人都在摸索衡量AI应用成效的正确指标。普遍共识是令牌本身并非理想指标，人们更倾向于业务成果（理想状态下）。但针对业务成果（如增收或增产），必须深入每个具体工作流才能判断AI是否成功实现转型，因此自上而下的管理难度更大。  
* 越来越多观点认为企业将生活在多模型并存的世界。人们对能基于成本或性能将工作负载路由至不同模型（前端或开放权重）的中间层兴趣浓厚（尽管实际应用尚处早期）。同时企业也在探索哪些任务直接交给模型处理，哪些应剥离为横向系统与上下文，以便随时替换任意系统。  
* 推动AI应用与落地的人才仍是重大议题和挑战。许多人认为由于外部相关人才短缺，必须通过内部培训解决。顺带一提，这对擅长企业级智能体部署与管理的人而言仍是巨大机遇，因为多数公司都在寻觅这类技能。  
* AI的最佳应用场景往往是彻底改变工作方式，而非简单替代现有流程并提升效率。各企业正根据行业特性探索自身路径，这通常既是AI最令人振奋的应用，也是最具高回报潜力的方向。  
近期还探讨了更多议题，但总体而言，变革浪潮汹涌，未来仍有无限可能。
morningIntake.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: A small percentage of useful data is on the open web available to all models for training or for agents to operate with. Most of it lives inside of organizations and often is in legacy systems, in people’s heads, or is fragmented across the enterprise. It’s the marketing plans, product roadmaps, development practices, contracts, financial data, strategies, and general corporate knowledge that every company operates off of. Whether it’s for training a model or used as context for agents, this data will increasingly be more valuable over time. The ability to get the right data to agents to operate on, securely, will be a defining characteristic of how companies operate and compete in the future. Effective use of AI will in the economy will largely come down to the companies that are able to have agents best understand their business and make the right decisions on their behalf. Data actually is the new oil.
morningIntake.translation: 公开网络上可供所有模型训练或代理操作的有用数据只占很小一部分。大部分数据存在于组织内部，往往分散在遗留系统、员工头脑中，或是零散地分布在整个企业里。这些数据包括每家公司的营销计划、产品路线图、开发实践、合同、财务数据、战略以及通用企业知识。无论是用于模型训练，还是作为代理的上下文，这些数据随着时间的推移将变得越来越有价值。能否安全地将正确数据提供给代理进行操作，将成为未来公司运营和竞争的一个决定性特征。在经济活动中，人工智能的有效运用，很大程度上将取决于哪些公司能让代理最深入地理解其业务，并代表公司做出正确决策。数据确实是新的石油。

morningIntake.text: imo this is the most impt part of anthropic's J-space paper today. it's a two-parter: 1) ant proved that they can do "brain surgery" interventions into reasoning to change topics midstream* 2) THE MODEL IS ABLE TO DETECT WHAT INTERVENTION WAS DONE - close cousin to eval awareness** *control > correlation - this convincingly demonstrates understanding **this was prompted awareness... surely @mlpowered's team also tried to eval unprompted awareness but i didn't see evidence of that
morningIntake.translation: 依我看，这是今天Anthropic的J-space论文中最关键的部分。它包含两层意思：1) Anthropic证明了他们能对推理过程进行“脑外科手术”式干预，从而在中途改变话题*；2) 模型能够检测到实施了何种干预——这与评估意识**密切相关。*控制优于相关性——这令人信服地证明了理解能力**这是提示引发的意识……想必@mlpowered的团队也尝试过评估非提示引发的意识，但我没看到相关证据。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Great post on how to think about open source AI and the applied AI layer. Two things will always be true in AI. Frontier intelligence will likely remain at the forefront of solving brand new used cases and often be used for orchestrating and planning of any type of complex workflow. At the same time, as use-cases become mature and predictable in an enterprise context, you can begin to peel off some set of the tokens to either lower cost open or closed models *or* models that are trained for the task at hand. Doing this too early in the adoption curve of any new use-case doesn’t make sense as you don’t know what you’re optimizing for, which is why there will always be a bit of a lag here. This process can essentially run on forever as there is no end for both the benefits of frontier intelligence or tuned models. This is why the spend and token volume for both approaches will continue to go up for years to come as we’re still early on both. Again, the only reason this dynamic is possible though is because of the applied AI layer that can effectively eval their workflows in a specific domain, choose a mixture of models to solve tasks, and have enough scale to eventually train their own models for their purposes.
morningIntake.translation: 关于如何思考开源AI与应用AI层的精彩文章。前沿智能与定制模型这两件事在AI领域将始终成立。前沿智能可能持续引领全新用例的解决，并常用于编排规划各类复杂工作流。与此同时，当企业场景中的用例趋于成熟可预测时，您就可以开始剥离部分令牌，转而采用成本更低的开源或闭源模型，或是针对特定任务训练的模型。在任何新用例的采用曲线上过早实施这一策略都毫无意义，因为您尚不清楚优化目标，这也解释了为何此处总会存在些许滞后。这个过程本质上可以永续运行，因为前沿智能与调优模型的优势都没有终点。正因如此，两种方案的开支与令牌消耗量在未来数年仍将持续攀升——我们在两方面都仍处于早期阶段。需要重申的是，这种动态之所以可能实现，完全得益于应用AI层能够有效评估特定领域的工作流、选择混合模型来完成任务，并具备足够规模最终训练出专属模型。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: The battle in AI is shaping up to be a battle for context. Everything in AI is about making sure that agents are effective as possible. That effectiveness comes down to whether the agent has the right domain expertise, access to the right context and tools to work with, and are involved in workflow in a way that users can easily interact with, review its work, and incorporate it into the rest of the process. As a consequence, the platforms that are able to capture and leverage the best and most context within their agents —and be able to pick the right models for the task- will be the place where agents do their best work. You can just look at coding agents, legal agents, or support agents as examples of what this looks like at scale. This is why the applied AI layer has a lot more value than just being an LLM wrapper. The ability to organize the critical knowledge for the work being done, and maintain this knowledge in a governed way where only the right people and agents have access, and the ability to improve the context for agents more and more over time, is critical. Over time, this layer will be able to route work between a variety of models, leveraging frontier intelligence for planning and orchestration and review, and a mix of lower cost models (open or closed) for the large volume of work between these tasks. The applied layer is also in a good position to train and develop its own models as well that are purpose built for their domains. Never good to bet against the bitter lesson, but equally taking a near frontier base model and post training it for just one type of domain work can -in many cases- lower costs or deliver better performance for certain tasks. Finally, this applied layer is also where most of the change management of the workflow will need to occur. This is why FDEs are so important at the applied layer, because this is the point where the customer needs to have specific business problem solved by a particular vendor. Whichever companies can solve that completely in an end-to-end fashion will have the greatest moats. As each day goes on, we’re learning more about what the likely long term market dynamics will look like in AI.
morningIntake.translation: 人工智能领域的竞争，正逐渐演变为一场对上下文的争夺。AI的一切都围绕着如何让智能体尽可能高效运转。这种高效性，取决于智能体是否具备正确的领域专业知识，能否获取恰当的上下文和工具，以及它们融入工作流程的方式是否能让用户轻松交互、审核其成果，并将其整合到后续流程中。因此，那些能在智能体中捕获并利用最优质、最丰富上下文的平台——并且能为任务选择最合适模型的平台——将成为智能体发挥最佳效能的舞台。只需看看编程智能体、法律智能体或客服智能体，就能明白这种规模化应用的模样。这正是应用层AI的价值远不止于大语言模型包装壳的原因。能够为手头工作组织关键知识，并以受管控的方式维护这些知识，确保只有合适的人员和智能体才能访问，同时随着时间推移不断优化智能体的上下文，这种能力至关重要。久而久之，这一层将能在多种模型间调度工作，利用前沿智能进行规划、编排和审核，并在大量任务间隙使用成本更低的模型组合（无论是开源还是闭源）。应用层也处于有利位置，能够训练和开发专为其领域量身定制的自有模型。虽然永远不应低估“苦涩的教训”，但在许多情况下，采用接近前沿的基础模型，并针对单一领域工作进行后训练，往往能降低成本或提升特定任务的表现。最后，应用层也是工作流程中大部分变革管理需要发生的地方。这就是为什么全栈领域专家在应用层如此重要，因为正是在这个节点，客户需要由特定供应商解决具体的业务问题。任何能以端到端方式彻底解决这些问题的公司，都将构筑起最深的护城河。日复一日，我们正逐渐看清AI领域可能呈现的长期市场格局。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: The deployment of AI in the enterprise beyond just interacting with a chatbot will unequivocally take real work to align AI systems to the underlying business processes they’re involved in and drive the desired outcomes. Most workflows weren’t designed for AI agents to just drop into. Workflows today in the enterprise deal with fragmented data, legacy software systems that agents can’t connect with, institutional instead of documented knowledge, and more. To deploy agents reliably at scale you need to get data cleaned up, modernize IT systems, figure out evals, drive change management for the new end state process, and so on. This also involves designing where humans remain in the loop (which will mean entirely new ways people interact with the workflows), and figuring out what a company’s new IP looks like. This is why so many applied AI companies are expanding FDE efforts and launching deploycos, and why the FDE role will be one of the most critical jobs in tech going forward. There’s a tremendous amount of work to be done on this front.
morningIntake.translation: 在企业中部署人工智能，远不止与聊天机器人互动那么简单，这无疑需要付出切实努力，使AI系统与所涉及的基础业务流程对齐，并推动实现预期成果。大多数工作流程并非为AI代理直接嵌入而设计。如今企业中的工作流程面临数据碎片化、代理无法连接的遗留软件系统、依赖机构知识而非文档化知识等诸多问题。要大规模可靠部署代理，就需要清理数据、现代化IT系统、制定评估方案、推动新终端状态流程的变革管理等等。这还包括设计人类在流程中保留的环节（这将意味着人们与工作流程交互的全新方式），以及明确公司新的知识产权形态。正因如此，众多应用型AI公司正在扩大实地工程部署工作并推出部署公司，而实地工程部署角色将成为未来科技领域最关键的职位之一。在这方面，还有大量工作有待完成。

morningIntake.text: Thought I’d share a fun activity I did with my 8-year-old daughter and Codex today: 1. She drew a picture of a dragon. 2. We uploaded her drawing to Codex and used image gen to make a few more dragon poses. She used voice to give Codex feedback until we got the result below. 3. We sent it to customstickers(dot)com to print sticker sheets (it's about $20 for 10 sheets). Now she can share her stickers with her friends when they arrive in the mail next week. Try it if you have kids stuck at home this summer :)
morningIntake.translation:
想和大家分享今天我和8岁女儿用Codex做的一个有趣活动：  
1. 她画了一幅龙的图画。  
2. 我们把她的画上传到Codex，用图像生成功能做了几个不同姿态的龙。她用语音给Codex反馈，直到得到下面这个结果。  
3. 我们把图发到customstickers(dot)com打印贴纸页（大约$20印10张）。  
下周贴纸寄到后，她就能和朋友们分享了。如果你家孩子这个夏天也宅在家，不妨试试 :)

morningIntake.name: Guillermo Rauch
morningIntake.role: vercel CEO
morningIntake.text: We've been explaining AI Gateway as a C̶o̶n̶t̶e̶n̶t̶ Token Delivery Network. Like a CDN for AI models. One great feature of CDNs is the ability to dynamically re-route or deny traffic without redeployment. When Fable was suddenly retired, we worried about production workloads depending on it. Even Fable aside, models get retired quite often as GPU capacity is heavily contested. Yet our data shows people still having happy production traffic of older model versions! We're solving this now with AI Gateway Rules. e.g.: you can now rewrite model "routes" on the fly! 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕 𝚊𝚒-𝚐𝚊𝚝𝚎𝚠𝚊𝚢 𝚛𝚞𝚕𝚎𝚜 𝚊𝚍𝚍 \ --𝚝𝚢𝚙𝚎 𝚛𝚎𝚠𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚎 \ --𝚜𝚘𝚞𝚛𝚌𝚎 𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚏𝚊𝚋𝚕𝚎-𝟻 \ --𝚍𝚎𝚜𝚝𝚒𝚗𝚊𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗 𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚘𝚙𝚞𝚜-𝟻 This adds to the repertoire of capabilities and techniques AI Gateway brings to the table to recover lost tokens. If you drop tokens, you lose revenue and customers 💸 Kudos to @rtaneja_ @crowprose @shaper – we went from CLI design to production-grade capability very quickly!
morningIntake.translation:
我们一直将AI网关比作一个~~内容~~令牌分发网络，就像AI模型的CDN。CDN的一大亮点在于无需重新部署即可动态重路由或拦截流量。当Fable突然退役时，我们担心依赖它的生产工作负载会受影响。即便抛开Fable不谈，由于GPU资源竞争激烈，模型退役其实相当常见。但我们的数据显示，仍有用户在使用旧版模型进行愉快的生产流量！现在，我们通过AI网关规则来解决这个问题。例如：您现在可以即时重写模型"路由"！  
𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕 𝚊𝚒-𝚐𝚊𝚝𝚎𝚠𝚊𝚢 𝚛𝚞𝚕𝚎𝚜 𝚊𝚍𝚍 \  
--𝚝𝚢𝚙𝚎 𝚛𝚎𝚠𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚎 \  
--𝚜𝚘𝚞𝚛𝚌𝚎 𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚏𝚊𝚋𝚕𝚎-𝟻 \  
--𝚍𝚎𝚜𝚝𝚒𝚗𝚊𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗 𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚘𝚙𝚞𝚜-𝟻  
这进一步丰富了AI网关挽回丢失令牌的手段和技术储备。令牌一旦丢失，您就会损失收入和客户💸  
感谢@rtaneja_ @crowprose @shaper——我们从CLI设计到生产级能力落地，速度非常快！

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Things seem to be ending up in a better spot with Fable, and presumably GPT-5.6 next. What we have now is the initial precedent for what frontier model releases (or at least those that have significant coding and cyber capabilities) could look like going forward. This would presumably apply to bio and other categories of risk that have been identified by AI safety groups. From the Anthropic post: “3. A shared industry framework. Although we have reached a constructive resolution, these events have made clear that the industry needs a consistent way to assess and fix potential “jailbreaks” of AI models (techniques that bypass a model’s safeguards).2 A shared standard for judging the severity of a given jailbreak would help AI developers triage new findings as they arise, launch highly-capable models with greater safety, and communicate the level of risk consistently to government and industry partners. Together with Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and other Glasswing partners, we’ve started to develop such a framework, and we outline it below. 4. Deeper government collaboration. We’re also strengthening our level of collaboration with the US government on new pre-release testing, information sharing, and research collaboration. We describe this deeper collaboration in the final section.” It’s been a messy process to get here, but at least there’s some semblance of a framework that could be practical. The only note of caution here would be that there’s a lot of subjectivity that goes into various risks and their actual levels of exploitability in practice. We’re likely going to be living with a framework that requires heavy judgment and back and forth between labs and the government for major releases. The best we can hope for is that this is a relatively efficient process, and hopefully has ways of being sped up for incremental version updates in models. It would be a bad outcome if every release after this level of threshold of capability required the same review process, and we don’t get the same rate of breakthroughs we’ve been seeing.
morningIntake.translation: 《神鬼寓言》的情况似乎在好转，接下来大概就是GPT-5.6了。我们目前所看到的，是前沿模型发布（或至少是那些具备显著编程和网络能力的模型）未来可能呈现的初步先例。这大概也适用于人工智能安全组织所识别的生物及其他风险类别。引用Anthropic的博文：“3. 共享的行业框架。尽管我们达成了建设性的解决方案，但这些事件清楚地表明，行业需要一种一致的方式来评估和修复人工智能模型潜在的‘越狱’问题（即绕过模型安全防护的技术）。2 一个用于判断特定越狱严重程度的共享标准，将有助于人工智能开发者在发现新问题时进行分类，更安全地推出高能力模型，并向政府和行业合作伙伴一致地传达风险级别。我们已与亚马逊、微软、谷歌及其他Glasswing合作伙伴着手开发这样一个框架，并在下文概述。4. 更深入的政府合作。我们也在加强与美国政府在新型发布前测试、信息共享和研究合作方面的协作。我们在最后一节描述了这种更深入的合作。”走到这一步的过程颇为混乱，但至少有了一个看似可行的框架雏形。这里唯一需要警惕的是，各种风险及其在实际中可被利用的程度，存在很大的主观性。对于重大发布，我们很可能将依赖一个需要大量判断以及实验室与政府之间反复沟通的框架。我们所能期望的最好结果，是这个过程相对高效，并有望在模型的增量版本更新时得以加速。如果在此能力阈值之后，每次发布都需要经历同样的审查流程，导致我们无法保持迄今所见的突破速度，那将是一个糟糕的结果。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: We've been running Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 through the Box AI Complex Work Eval, our agentic benchmark that puts models through real enterprise document work end-to-end. Sonnet 5 holds frontier-class quality on complex multi-step work and pulls ahead of Sonnet 4.6 in several core enterprise domains like Energy (+4.7pp), Retail (+4.4pp), and Professional Services (+2.6pp), and other spaces where unstructured data is heavily complex. Here are a few examples of wins compared to Sonnet 4.6 to get a sense of some of the more advanced reasoning capabilities in Sonnet 5: * Financing due diligence: It computed the company's liquidity and leverage ratios from the raw balance sheet, and caught that the source report's own stated debt-to-equity figure understated the leverage, flagging all three loan covenants as violated, not just the ones the document admitted. * Overhaul cost analysis: It scoped "total cost" to the company's own KPI definitions, correctly separating out Lost Production Cost because the guidance said to track it separately rather than naively summing every number on the sheet. It also caught and handled a broken reference cell in the spreadsheet. * SKU revenue analysis: On segmented sales data, it computed each product's contribution against the correct subcategory denominator, sidestepping the easy mistake of dividing by the category total, and flagged why no Pet-category SKU cracked the top 9. Sonnet 5 will be available in the Box AI Studio shortly for customers to build custom agents with.
morningIntake.translation:
我们一直在通过Box AI复杂工作评估（Box AI Complex Work Eval）测试Anthropic的Claude Sonnet 5，这是我们的智能体基准测试，让模型端到端地处理真实的企业文档工作。Sonnet 5在复杂的多步骤工作中保持了前沿级质量，并在能源（+4.7个百分点）、零售（+4.4个百分点）和专业服务（+2.6个百分点）等几个核心企业领域，以及其他非结构化数据极为复杂的场景中，领先于Sonnet 4.6。以下是与Sonnet 4.6相比的一些胜出示例，以便了解Sonnet 5中一些更高级的推理能力：  
* 融资尽职调查：它从原始资产负债表中计算了公司的流动性和杠杆比率，并发现源报告自身所述的债务股本比低估了杠杆水平，标记出所有三项贷款契约均被违反，而不仅仅是文件承认的那些。  
* 大修成本分析：它根据公司自身的KPI定义界定了“总成本”，正确地将停产损失成本分离出来，因为指南要求单独跟踪，而不是简单地将表格上的每个数字相加。它还发现并处理了电子表格中的一个损坏引用单元格。  
* SKU收入分析：在细分销售数据上，它针对正确的子类别分母计算了每个产品的贡献，避开了除以类别总额的常见错误，并标记了为何没有宠物类别的SKU进入前9名。  
Sonnet 5将很快在Box AI Studio中提供，供客户构建自定义智能体。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: More data is showing the opposite of what many people expected with AI adoption and jobs. Ramp found that the more AI adoption a company has the more their headcount grows. At Box, we recently did a survey of 1,600+ mid and large sized companies, and the findings were similar. 58% of respondents expected headcount to rise over the next three years. Interestingly, that figure climbs to 79% among the most mature adopters of AI. The more advanced AI adopters expected to grow their headcount at a greater rate in the future than others. Of course it's true that the companies that can afford to adopt AI the most are also the ones that likely are seeing growth in their business, leading to more headcount. So the point of the story isn't necessarily that by adopting AI you will inherently grow. *But* the most important takeaway is that the opposite is not proving out. The fears a couple years ago would have been that the companies adopting AI the most would be hiring fewer people. But in reality this is what actually you should expect to happen. If a company can get more customers because they use AI in sales for account or market intelligence, they hire more sales people not fewer. If you can build way more software than before, you end up hiring more engineers because the projects get bigger and you take on more. And so on.
morningIntake.translation: 更多数据表明，AI应用与就业的关系与许多人的预期恰恰相反。Ramp发现，企业AI应用程度越高，员工人数增长越快。在Box，我们最近对1600多家中大型企业进行了调查，结果与此类似。58%的受访者预计未来三年员工人数将增加。有趣的是，在AI应用最成熟的企业中，这一比例攀升至79%。AI应用越深入的企业，未来员工人数的预期增长率也越高。当然，能够大力投入AI的企业，往往也是业务增长较快的企业，这自然会带动员工人数增加。所以，这个故事的重点并不在于应用AI就一定会带来增长。*但*最重要的启示是，相反的情况并未出现。几年前人们担心，AI应用最多的企业会减少招聘。但实际上，这才是你应该预料到的结果。如果一家企业利用AI进行销售，获取客户或市场情报，从而赢得更多客户，他们就会招聘更多而非更少的销售人员。如果你能开发出比以前多得多的软件，最终你会雇佣更多工程师，因为项目规模会扩大，承接的任务也会增多。其他领域也是如此。

morningIntake.name: Madhu Guru
morningIntake.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
morningIntake.text: The biggest challenge for trad PMs trying to adapt to AI native building is a lack of magical thinking. A decade of frameworks, “agile” and metric obsession has led to constraint-first, incremental thinking. I used to combat this on my teams by asking them to imagine we had tech from 100 yrs in the future, the experiences it would enable and working backward from that. the ideas would always be far more creative. the funny thing is, that technology from the future is here. go build that magical thing!
morningIntake.translation: 传统产品经理在适应AI原生开发时面临的最大挑战，是缺乏天马行空的想象力。十年来对框架、"敏捷"和指标的痴迷，导致了以约束为先、渐进式的思维模式。我过去常在团队中对抗这种倾向，方法是让大家想象我们拥有100年后的未来科技，设想它能实现怎样的体验，再反向推导——这样产生的创意总是更具突破性。有趣的是，那种未来科技其实已经到来。去创造那些不可思议的事物吧！

morningIntake.name: Madhu Guru
morningIntake.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
morningIntake.text: What’s underappreciated is that the rise of strong open-weight models like GLM will actually strengthen Google’s position. more companies will now start experimenting with fine tuning open-weight models such as GLM and the value will accrue to the infra. enterprises want the flexibility to run and fine-tune open models on a managed platform with enterprise-grade reliability, security, and support. And Google Cloud is well positioned there. also don’t forget Google owns much of the compute stack.
morningIntake.translation: 常被忽视的一点是，像GLM这类强开源权重模型的崛起，实际上会巩固谷歌的地位。更多企业将开始尝试微调GLM等开源权重模型，而价值将流向基础设施层。企业希望在具备企业级可靠性、安全性和支持的托管平台上灵活运行和微调开源模型，而谷歌云在这方面占据优势。此外别忘了，谷歌还掌控着计算堆栈的很大一部分。

morningIntake.name: Thibault Sottiaux
morningIntake.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
morningIntake.text: Tons of improvements landed in Codex. - Handles super long threads smoothly. - Hoverable navigation rail for previewing and jumping between turns that feels just right. - Settings search covers more controls, with clearer appearance and host-filtering options and easier-to-find custom-provider settings. - Zoom-level changes no longer misalign tooltips, dialogs, menus, selection bubbles, drag previews, or autocomplete. - Copying into Slack preserves Markdown formatting such as bullets, bold text, code, and links; and large text pastes no longer freeze the UI. - And most importantly: a dedicated Pets panel.
morningIntake.translation:
Codex 迎来大量改进。  
- 流畅处理超长对话线程。  
- 可悬停的导航栏，用于预览和跳转对话轮次，手感恰到好处。  
- 设置搜索覆盖更多控件，外观更清晰，新增主机过滤选项，自定义服务商设置更易查找。  
- 缩放级别变化不再导致工具提示、对话框、菜单、选择气泡、拖拽预览或自动补全错位。  
- 复制到 Slack 时保留 Markdown 格式，如项目符号、粗体文本、代码和链接；粘贴大段文本不再导致界面卡顿。  
- 最重要的是：新增专属宠物面板。

morningIntake.name: Matt Turck
morningIntake.role: VC at FirstMarkCap. Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
morningIntake.text: Smart glasses and goggles, a history: Silicon Valley, 2013 (Google): “you really want this” Everyone: “no we don’t” Silicon Valley, 2016 (Microsoft): “ok but what if it’s for the enterprise” Enterprise: “maybe, but also, no” Silicon Valley, 2023 (Meta): “ok but what if they look normal and have AI” Everyone: “wait… maybe? … Actually, no” Silicon Valley, 2024 (Apple): “ok but what if it’s $3,499 and covers your whole face” Everyone: “absolutely not” Silicon Valley, 2026 (Snap): “ok but this time for real” Everyone: “we admire the persistence but still no”
morningIntake.translation:
智能眼镜与护目镜简史：  
硅谷，2013年（谷歌）：“你真的需要这个”  
众人：“不，我们不需要”  
硅谷，2016年（微软）：“好吧，但如果是面向企业呢”  
企业：“也许吧，但还是不要”  
硅谷，2023年（Meta）：“好吧，但如果它们看起来正常，还带AI呢”  
众人：“等等……也许？……其实，还是不要”  
硅谷，2024年（苹果）：“好吧，但如果它售价$3,499，还能遮住你整张脸呢”  
众人：“绝对不要”  
硅谷，2026年（Snap）：“好吧，但这次是认真的”  
众人：“我们钦佩这份坚持，但还是不要”

morningIntake.name: Swyx
morningIntake.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
morningIntake.text: An interesting way to take Noam at his word in regards to always keeping a constant inference budget for any eval reporting - is that open models have a lot more dollar per token mileage than closed model APIs. So anyone launching an open model today or situationally incentivized toward open models should obviously report thinking levels measured by dollar inference on popular inference providers, instead of by number of tokens on the x axis
morningIntake.translation: 诺姆关于在任何评估报告中始终保持恒定推理预算的说法，有一个有趣的解读方式——即开源模型在每美元可获取的token数量上，比闭源模型API更具性价比。因此，任何今天发布开源模型的人，或出于特定情境倾向于开源模型的人，显然应该以主流推理服务商上的美元推理成本来衡量思考层级（而非以x轴上的token数量作为衡量标准）。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: Some good best practices here on AI token cost optimization. None of these happens though without a deep understanding of the underlying work being done in a non-abstract way. The ultimate implication is that a layer between the work itself and the underlying intelligence needs to deeply understand your workflows, context, and business process. Now, each individual company doing this on their own is unlikely to be effective at scale, so as a consequence, this is effectively the playbook for any applied AI company right now. By evaling the models for the applied use cases, deeply understanding the domain, having tuned UX and features for the use case, and having the ability to support adoption and change (via FDEs), allow this layer to add a ton of value. And as a result, enterprises get higher ROI because you actually can get *more* intelligence per dollar by having optimal architecture and workflows. There will be many horizontal and vertical versions of this approach. Huge opportunity right now.
morningIntake.translation: 这里有一些关于AI令牌成本优化的优秀实践。然而，若不深入理解底层工作的非抽象方式，这些实践都无法实现。最终意味着，在工作本身与底层智能之间，需要有一个层面能够深刻理解你的工作流程、上下文和业务流程。现在，如果每家公司都独自进行这项工作，很难在大规模上取得成效，因此，这实际上就是当前任何应用型AI公司的操作手册。通过针对应用场景评估模型、深入理解领域、为用例调整用户体验和功能，并具备支持采纳和变革的能力（通过现场部署工程师），这一层面能够带来巨大价值。结果，企业能获得更高的投资回报率，因为通过优化架构和工作流程，你实际上可以用每一美元获得*更多*的智能。这种方法将出现许多横向和纵向的版本。目前存在巨大的机遇。

morningIntake.name: Guillermo Rauch
morningIntake.role: vercel CEO
morningIntake.text: Agents are particularly hard-to-debug software. For one, and by design, AI models behave in non-deterministic ways. Even two identical prompts don't always yield the same output. But agents are also complex distributed systems. They involve multiple steps of computation across functions and sandboxes, touching dozens of API services that can go down, rate limit you, etc. Nailing down observability out of the box for https://t.co/nDDXqUmOlD on Vercel was a key priority for the team, and the feedback so far has been 🔥↓
morningIntake.translation: 代理类软件尤其难以调试。一方面，AI模型本身具有非确定性行为——即便两次完全相同的提示词，也可能产生不同输出。但代理同时也是复杂的分布式系统：它们涉及跨函数和沙盒的多步计算，需要调用数十个可能宕机或触发速率限制的API服务。在Vercel上为https://t.co/nDDXqUmOlD实现开箱即用的可观测性，是团队的核心优先事项，而目前的反馈简直🔥↓

morningIntake.name: Nikunj Kothari
morningIntake.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
morningIntake.text: Now that the dust is settled, what’s really funny to me about the taste commentary is it’s coming from a LOT of people who never have built anything.. You can’t achieve (or refine) taste without being in the arena. It’s like a chef cooking and expecting their first dish to be good on the first go. But, when you give it a 100 iterations and maybe cook a 100 dishes, then the 101st one has imbued all the lessons from those 10,000 iterations. But, if you over fit without much variation, then people get tired of it since well they want something new as well. The best people are able to learn, imbue and innovate by breaking patterns consistently. It’ll be interesting to see how AI does the same - and unlike most people I think it has a fair shot at actually building taste. End rant.
morningIntake.translation: 尘埃落定后，关于品味的评论让我觉得真正好笑的是，这些评论来自很多从未真正建造过任何东西的人……不置身其中，你根本无法获得（或打磨）品味。这就像一位厨师做饭，指望第一道菜就能一次成功。但当你反复尝试100次，或许做了100道菜之后，第101道菜就会融入那10,000次迭代中的所有经验教训。然而，如果你过度拟合而缺乏变化，人们就会厌倦，因为他们也想要一些新东西。最优秀的人能够通过不断打破模式来学习、融入并创新。看看人工智能如何做到同样的事会很有趣——与大多数人不同，我认为它确实有相当的机会真正培养出品味。牢骚完毕。

morningIntake.name: Dan Shipper
morningIntake.role: ceo every
morningIntake.text: BREAKING: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol! As of today, by U.S. government directive, access is limited to only ~20 pre-approved companies and @every is not on the list. This appears to be a temporary situation while the government races to figure out a long-term policy for releasing frontier models with advanced capabilities. I understand and applaud the need for some government oversight in making American infrastructure resilient to cyberattacks and other potential new threats from the misuse of these models. However, I also strongly believe that widespread democratic access to frontier models is absolutely necessary to our country’s leading position in the AI race. It’s also critical for allowing American workers to keep pace with the new skills they need to be productive in this era. A world where advanced models are locked up only for use by the employees of AI giants and a select few companies is one where ambitious students, independent builders, and working professionals are denied the tools they need to learn, create, and compete to their fullest potential. Also, speaking for @every and the community of developers and writers who share early access with us: Our job is to test these tools early so that we can prepare Americans for how to use them to their fullest extent. If we lose access, we lose the ability to do that important job well. Thankfully, both OpenAI and the government seem to be working to make broad access available soon. We’ll be ready to vibe check when that happens :)
morningIntake.translation: 重磅消息：OpenAI 发布了 GPT-5.6 Sol！即日起，根据美国政府指令，该模型仅限约 20 家预先批准的公司使用，而 @every 并不在名单之列。这似乎是临时性安排，政府正加紧制定针对具备先进能力的前沿模型的长期发布政策。我理解并赞同政府需要实施一定监管，以增强美国基础设施抵御网络攻击及防范此类模型被滥用所带来的其他潜在新威胁。但我同样坚信，让前沿模型实现广泛的民主化使用，对于我国在人工智能竞赛中保持领先地位绝对至关重要。这也关系到美国劳动者能否及时掌握新时代所需的新技能，从而保持高效生产力。若先进模型仅被锁在 AI 巨头和少数几家公司的员工手中，那么胸怀大志的学生、独立开发者以及职场专业人士，都将失去学习、创造和充分施展才华所必需的工具。此外，我谨代表 @every 以及与我们共享早期访问权限的开发者与创作者社群发声：我们的职责是尽早测试这些工具，以便帮助美国民众做好充分准备，学会如何最大限度地运用它们。若失去访问权限，我们便无法出色完成这项重要使命。所幸的是，OpenAI 与政府似乎都在努力推动尽快实现广泛开放。届时，我们定会第一时间进行体验评测 :)

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: AI regulation is far less simple than it looks. It’s prisoners dilemma at insane scale. In theory if all leading AI labs globally agreed to the same process of review and slow down, then we’d get frontier intelligence at similar rates and it diffuses relatively evenly. If the US remains at the frontier at all times, and has heavy regulation on the release of intelligence then we end up with an economic and geopolitical edge because we can control who has access to frontier intelligence. If we delay model releases, however, and another player - specifically China - doesn’t slow down and has equally strong models (not now but soon?) then our delays end up advantaging their models and eventually their tech stack. Now, the US could ban these models, but that actually only puts the US at a steeper disadvantage because other countries won’t have those bans. Then, from a relative competitiveness standpoint the US has now fallen behind even though it started in front. So, none of this is as simple as it looks. At some point it’s a simple bet of can closed models remain at the frontier in perpetuity or is there a risk of any other player or market catching up or just not falling behind.
morningIntake.translation: 人工智能监管远非表面看起来那么简单，这本质上是一场规模惊人的囚徒困境。理论上，如果全球所有顶尖AI实验室都同意采用相同的审查流程并放慢研发速度，那么我们就能以相近的速度获得前沿智能，且其扩散会相对均衡。若美国始终处于技术前沿，并对智能技术的发布实施严格监管，我们就能通过控制前沿智能的获取权限来获得经济和地缘政治优势。然而，如果我们推迟模型发布，而其他参与者——特别是中国——并未放缓脚步，且拥有同样强大的模型（目前尚未实现，但或许很快？），那么我们的延迟反而会助长其模型优势，最终壮大其技术生态。美国固然可以禁用这些模型，但这只会让美国陷入更被动的境地，因为其他国家不会实施此类禁令。这样一来，从相对竞争力的角度看，即便美国最初领先，最终也会落后。因此，这一切远非表面那么简单。归根结底，这不过是一场简单赌局：封闭模型能否永远保持前沿地位，还是存在其他参与者或市场迎头赶上甚至不被甩开的风险。

morningIntake.name: Aaron Levie
morningIntake.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
morningIntake.text: We now have de facto AI regulation. It’s not obvious why from here on out models that have certain levels of capability or are trained on certain compute sizes won’t have to be reviewed by the government before release. Realistically, as AI models became more and more powerful this was going to be inevitable (I think it’s too early, but here we are). So now it’s mostly just interesting to think about the implications and scenarios from here. A few would be: * America gets to control who gets access to frontier intelligence and when. This generally works as long as we remain at the frontier at all times and don’t have a risk of being surpassed. At the moment we have a clear lead in frontier intelligence so this is a good bet, but lots of motivated parties would love to change that. * This likely creates backlog of AI releases which means that we will see less rapid fire back and forth jumps in model progress. Bull/fine case is that we just get bigger step functions per release at a slower rate and we end up at the same point we would have. Bear case is those incremental smaller jumps were necessary for the continued flywheel of innovation. * Other countries likely have even more incentive to at least hedge their bets with sovereign AI strategies so aren’t dependent on access to US AI all times. Previously this was relatively moot because the alternative wasn’t good enough, but that could change out of necessity and what we’re seeing in China. * Open weights obviously a big winner here as it becomes what likely sovereign AI gets built out on, and what (for now) can still be released to the market without the same controls. One interesting question would be how regulation eventually extends to open models, which would have its own set of long term consequences. Anyway some big updates to everyone’s mental models of AI regulation as a result of the capabilities we’re now seeing in AI. Wild times.
morningIntake.translation:
我们如今已有了事实上的AI监管。从今往后，具备特定能力水平或基于特定算力规模训练的模型，在发布前很可能需要接受政府审查，这背后的原因并不难理解。现实来看，随着AI模型日益强大，这种局面本就难以避免（我认为为时过早，但事已至此）。所以现在，思考由此产生的各种影响和情景，反倒成了最值得玩味的事。其中几点包括：  
* 美国得以掌控谁能获取前沿智能、以及何时获取。只要我们能始终站在前沿、没有被他国赶超的风险，这套机制大体就能奏效。目前我们在前沿智能领域优势明显，因此这算是个稳妥的赌注，但许多雄心勃勃的势力正渴望改变这一格局。  
* 这很可能导致AI发布出现积压，意味着模型进展中那种快速交替的跳跃式迭代会减少。乐观/理想的情况是，每次发布带来的能力跃升幅度更大，只是节奏放缓，最终我们仍能抵达原本会达到的同一节点。悲观的情况则是，那些渐进式的小幅跃迁，对于维持创新的持续飞轮本是必不可少的。  
* 其他国家很可能更有动力至少通过主权AI战略来对冲风险，以免在所有时刻都依赖美国的AI供应。此前这基本无关紧要，因为替代方案不够好，但出于现实需要，以及我们在中国看到的情况，这种局面可能改变。  
* 开放权重模型显然是这里的大赢家，它很可能成为主权AI构建的基础，并且（就目前而言）仍能在不受同等管控的情况下向市场发布。一个有趣的问题是，监管最终将如何延伸到开放模型，而这会带来一系列长期后果。  
总之，鉴于我们如今在AI身上看到的能力，每个人对AI监管的思维模型都迎来了重大更新。真是疯狂的时代。

morningIntake.text: Scaling Laws, Carefully
morningIntake.content: Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases predictably as we scale up model size $N$, dataset size $D$, and compute $C$, following a power-law curve, which appears as a straight line on a log-log p
morningIntake.contentTranslation: 缩放定律是深度学习中最关键的经验性发现之一。其形式简洁明了：训练损失 $L$ 会随着模型规模 $N$、数据集大小 $D$ 和计算量 $C$ 的扩大而可预测地降低，遵循幂律曲线，在对数-对数坐标图上呈现为一条直线。
morningIntake.translation: 缩放定律，需谨慎
